markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thrower's Bandolier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2313)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bandolier is covered in straps and pouches capable of holding up to 2 Bulk of one-handed [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapons. A _thrower's bandolier_ has a [_+1 weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) rune etched into it, and it can be etched with runes as though it were a one-handed thrown weapon. When you invest the _thrower's bandolier_, you can attune it to all the weapons sheathed in it (this ends any previous attunements made with the bandolier). Whenever you draw a weapon from the bandolier, the bandolier's runes are replicated onto that weapon. Any runes already on the weapon are suppressed, and any runes previously replicated to a different weapon in this way are removed, returning it to normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** All weapons attuned to the bandolier, not including any weapons you're currently wielding, return to the bandolier.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thrower's Bandolier

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clarity Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2314)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The counteract check from a pair of clarity goggles has a counteract rank of half the item’s level rounded up._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Clarity goggles_ feature faceted lenses that filter your surroundings from several slightly different angles at once, giving you a sharper picture of them. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on your environment and the creatures around you to see them as they really are. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check using your Perception bonus against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) effect created by a 3rd-rank or lower spell or a creature of 8th level or lower. You must be able to see the illusion, and it must be within 60 feet. If the check succeeds, you see through the illusion for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clarity Goggles (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

**Price** 2,450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The goggles grant a +2 item bonus. They work against effects created by 6th-rank or lower spells or a creature of 13th level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clarity Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

**Price** 32,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The goggles grant a +3 item bonus. They work against effects created by 9th-rank or lower spells or a creature of 19th level or lower.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mirror Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2315)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These goggles feature highly reflective lenses. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks and to saving throws against [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet targets you with a visual effect;  **Effect** You turn your head to reflect aspects of the triggering effect back at its creator. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save as it becomes disoriented by this reflection. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mirror Goggles (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 135 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mirror Goggles (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the DC is 30.

level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mirror Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and the DC is 40.

</title>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

These goggles feature highly reflective lenses. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual Perception checks and to saving throws against [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet targets you with a visual effect;  **Effect** You turn your head to reflect aspects of the triggering effect back at its creator. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save as it becomes disoriented by this reflection. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Inquiry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anything viewed through these thin spectacles looks crisp and clear, and the earpieces accentuate sounds around you. You gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The spectacles key in on someone to show you their social cues in perfect clarity. Choose a creature you can see. You gain a +3 item bonus on Perception checks you make to use [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) against that creature. This benefit lasts until you Activate the item again in this way.

 If you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), you can use this activation as part of the [Devise a Stratagem](/Actions.aspx?ID=2813) action (even if you’re doing it as a free action). Alternatively, you can use it as part of the [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811) activity, even though you can’t see the creature. You must choose the same creature you chose for Devise a Stratagem or Pursue a Lead.

**Craft Requirements** You are an investigator.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Inquiry

[Spectacles of Piercing Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2317)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With lenses set in a silver frame, _spectacles of piercing_ sight grant you a +3 item bonus to visual Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision can pierce through solid materials up to 20 feet away as if looking at something in normal light even if no illumination is available. You can see through up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. A thin sheet of lead blocks this vision entirely.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Piercing Sight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accompaniment Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2318)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 147

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lush velvet capelet is embroidered with images of musicians playing a wide variety of instruments. The images animate when you make art, remaining embroidered, but smoothly unknitting and reknitting in time with your performance. The figures play music that perfectly accompanies your instrument, voice, or movements, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a bard [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=31) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You peel the musicians from the garment and fling them around you. The cloak casts a 4th-rank [_phantom crowd_](/Spells.aspx?ID=968) spell (DC 28); each of the 10-foot squares must be adjacent to you. The crowd looks like the musicians on the garment and continues to accompany your Performance checks. You can Sustain this effect as described in the spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=3).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Accompaniment Cloak

<traits>

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

Each _bloodline robe_ has a design that befits a particular [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) bloodline, depicting creatures of that bloodline or using styles common among them. You gain a +2 item bonus to each of your bloodline skills.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're benefiting from your blood magic effect; **Effect** You're [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) on your next turn. You can use the extra action only as part of Casting a bloodline spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a sorcerer bloodline spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a sorcerer of the bloodline tied to the robe.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodline Robe

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148 **Price** 1,400 gp **Usage** worn bracers **Bulk** L --- [Champions](/Classes.aspx?ID=4) adorn these bracers with the symbol of their deity or the text of the tenets they follow. While they're clasped around your forearms, reassuring focus and devotion flow into you through them. Each time you spend a Focus Point to cast a devotion spell, your divine ally gains a benefit until the start of your next turn, depending on its type.
  • **Blessed Armament** The bracers hold your weapon in place. You gain a +2 item bonus against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) you of your blessed armament.
  • **Blessed Shield** While raised, the shield grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) if you’re [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), resistance 10 to holy if you’re unholy, or resistance 5 to holy and unholy if you’re neither.
  • **Blessed Swiftness** The bonus to Speed is +10 feet.
**Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a champion devotion spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost. **Craft Requirements** You are a champion.
name: Bracers of Devotion

[Cloister Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Explorer's Clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The most devoted, [cloistered clerics](/Doctrines.aspx?ID=2) wear a _cloister robe_. Decorations symbolic of a specific deity adorn the robe, and the robe's colors and the complexity of its construction fit the deity's outlook. The robe serves as a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of that deity, and it doesn't need to be wielded to provide that benefit.

 The robe is _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2)_ and grants a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks. The robe doesn't grant any benefits to a wearer who doesn't worship the deity tied to the robe. In addition, when you cast a domain spell from one of the deity's domains, you gain resistance to damage from [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells until the end of your next turn. This resistance is equal to half the robe's level.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) who worships the deity tied to the robe.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Rune Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2322)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148, [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 75

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _dragon rune bracelet_ is a gold bangle formed around the scale of a famous [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50). The bracelet is etched with esoteric symbols or words in [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) that indicate kinship with dragons. As many types of _dragon rune bracelet_ exist as there are types of dragons, though bracelets associated with [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or [rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) dragons have the same rarity as the dragon.

 While wearing a _dragon rune bracelet_, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks while interacting with dragons of the same type as the bracelet's scale (such as [red dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168)  or [cloud dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=176)). You also gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects from any kind of dragon.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a sorcerer of a draconic bloodline.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Rune Bracelet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidolon Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2323)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though it appears to be one elegant piece, an _eidolon cape_ consists of a mantle for your shoulders and a detachable cape. The cape is designed to resemble your [eidolon](Eidolons.aspx), either with a direct likeness or with features reminiscent of your eidolon. For instance, if your eidolon is a [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), the cape might depict a stylized dragon, or it might have a pattern of colorful scales with gold trim. An eidolon cape features the sigil you share with your eidolon prominently in its design. You gain a +2 item bonus to the skill that matches your eidolon's tradition: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) for [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) for [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) for [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134).

 When you [Manifest your Eidolon](/Actions.aspx?ID=757), the cape transforms into the eidolon, and when the eidolon unmanifests, it turns back into the cape. If the cape is attached to the mantle, it comes free immediately, and the eidolon appears adjacent to you as normal. If not, the eidolon appears in a space adjacent to the cape. With the cape detached, the mantle is still light Bulk, and the whole cloak remains invested. If the cape is out of range (beyond 100 feet, typically) or there's not enough open space for the eidolon to manifest, your attempt to Manifest your Eidolon fails.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a summoner link spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [summoner](/Classes.aspx?ID=18).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eidolon Cape

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hexing Jar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2324)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 148

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dangling from a simple cord, a _hexing jar_ houses rich soil. When a [witch](/Classes.aspx?ID=16) who has invested the jar wears it overnight, a miniature thing grows from the soil. Your patron chooses the form, commonly including glowing mushrooms, venus flytraps, mandragora roots, or undead hands reaching up. The thing whispers secrets it learned from your patron, giving you a +2 item bonus to your patron skill.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a witch [hex](/Traits.aspx?ID=324) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You roll a critical success on an attack roll with a witch spell, or your target rolls a critical failure on its saving throw against a witch spell or hex you cast;  **Effect** The thing in the jar becomes more energetic—glowing, dancing, rapping on the glass, or some other action appropriate to its appearance. It encourages you until the start of your next turn, granting you and your familiar a +1 status bonus to AC and saving throws and a +2 status bonus against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects.

**Craft Requirements** You are a witch.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hexing Jar

As you move your body, qi flows into _mala beads of foresight_ you wear and have invested, making them one with your life force. In their usual form, beads are spheres of wood, but versions customized to different martial orders are common. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) monk qi spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You have just [Refocused](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by meditating;  **Effect** While meditating, you searched your feelings for a portent of the future. You're affected by an [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) with divine qi spells.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of Uncanny Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2326)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A thin wooden mask carved in the shape of a skull, monstrous face, or eerily featureless visage, a mask of uncanny breath fully covers your face. While wearing it, each breath you take feels cool and pure, perfectly flowing to feed your qi. You gain resistance 10 to [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and can breathe in an airless or toxic environment. When you breathe in, fragments of bizarre knowledge flow through you, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120) monk qi spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike hits a creature that breathes;  **Effect** The mask contorts and inhales, sucking breath from your target's lungs. The target falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) but doesn't fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) or drop what it's holding. It wakes up at the end of your turn if it hasn't been woken up already.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) with occult qi spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mask of Uncanny Breath

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Master Magus Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2327)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Elegant jewelry such as the _master magus ring_ adorns experienced [magi](/Classes.aspx?ID=17). Each ring has a significant metal and symbol to represent a particular hybrid study, such as a heavy iron ring with an icon of a mountain for [inexorable iron](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=1), or glittering silver with a shield-like emblem for [sparkling targe](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=3). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a magus conflux spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ring transports you and any items you're wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space you can see within a range equal to your Speed. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container—the activation fails and is used.

**Craft Requirements** You are a magus.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Master Magus Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mind's Light Circlet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2328)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gemstones of many colors adorn the silver of a _mind's light circlet_. When you're charged with mental power, the jewels scintillate with light, with different gems resonating based on your emotions. If you have at least 1 Focus Point, the gems cast [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) in a 10-foot radius. When you amp a spell, the light increases to [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) until the start of your next turn.

 You gain a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks. You also gain the following [amp](/Traits.aspx?ID=425), which you can apply to any of your psi cantrips that have a target or area, much like an amp gained from a feat.

 **Amp** You transfer some of the magical luminescence from the mind's light circlet to one of the creatures. Choose a creature targeted by the spell or in its area. Until the start of your next turn, that creature sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) and can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). If the creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's concealed while alight, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to use a psychic amp. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [psychic](/Classes.aspx?ID=21).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mind's Light Circlet

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oracular Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2329)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Patterns themed to your curse cover your _oracular crown_. As your curse worsens, the appearance of the crown changes, introducing extreme angles, stronger colors, or other indications of the intensity of your curse. Similarly, it gets closer to its natural form when you reduce the effects of your curse. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an oracle revelation spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=800) value is 1 or higher; **Effect** You regain 3d8 Hit Points. The amount of healing increases to 5d8 if you’re cursebound 2, 7d8 if you’re cursebound 3, or 9d8 if you’re cursebound 4. If you have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) ability, this activation has the [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) trait instead of the healing and vitality traits.

**Craft Requirements** You are an [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=14).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oracular Crown

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Unassuming in appearance, these slippers indicate their nature only with a signature strip of yellow stitching. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You move like the wind, with precision and speed. You Stride up to 60 feet; this movement doesn't trigger reactions. When you stop, if you've moved at least 30 feet from where you started, you release a thunderous 5-foot emanation that deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that critically fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

</column>

---

A small, feathered wing is attached to either side of this ornate brass helmet. A visor on the front lowers to cover your face. While wearing the _cassisian helmet_, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC and saves against [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures and effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Lowering the visor, you send out eye beams that deal your choice of 2d6 cold or fire damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to all creatures in a 15-foot line.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cassisian Helmet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of the Companion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2334)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151, [Pathfinder #170: Spoken on the Song Wind](/Sources.aspx?ID=97) pg. 75

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stories tell of a king who once loved his subjects so much he was willing to give his own life energy for them, using an object like the _crown of the companion_. Whether true or not, this majestic wooden crown bears elaborate carvings depicting that tale with images of a regal figure giving increasingly of themself to a throng of needy subjects. While wearing this crown, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You bow to an ally within 30 feet, creating a magical bond between the two of you as if you cast [_share life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) targeting the ally. The link remains even if you move more than 30 feet away from them. At the end of the spell’s duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crown of the Companion

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 151 **Price** 220 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Made of materials scavenged from wild places, a _druid's crown_ can be rebuilt for a variety of benefits. The crown grants you a +1 item bonus to a skill, and can be activated to cast a spell, both depending on the material used to build the crown, as listed on the table below. If you invest and wear [_living mantle_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3092) along with the crown, the crown's item bonus increases by 1 and its spell's DC rises to 27. You can invest this item only if you're a [druid](/Classes.aspx?ID=6). When you do, as a 10-minute activity that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, you can disassemble and rebuild the crown with different materials, changing its item bonus and spell accordingly. ##
**Material****Item Bonus****Spell**
Antlers[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)[_Enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102)
Flowers[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)[_Animal allies_](/Spells.aspx?ID=863)
Leaves[Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)[_One with Plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) <%END>
 **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crown casts its spell at 2nd rank (DC 20). **Craft Requirements** You are a druid.
name: Druid's Crown

[Headbands of Translocation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2336)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 152, [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 74

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Headbands of translocation_ are silk bands that come in pairs and usually feature a prominent symbol of a nation or team. If both wearers Invest their headbands at the same time and think of the same symbol while doing so, both headbands change to display that symbol until they are removed. If you both have invested your headbands, you can [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) each other without taking an action to prepare, and when you roll a critical failure when attempting to Aid an ally with a paired headband, you get a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You remove your headband, which teleports you to a space adjacent to the other Invested wearer's location, provided you are within 1 mile of each other.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Headbands of Translocation

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Instinct Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2338)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 152

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _instinct crown_ is a magical headpiece imbued with the essence of instincts that [barbarians](/Classes.aspx?ID=2) draw upon in combat. Each crown is fashioned to represent the instinct it's tied to, such as a wolf's head for an [animal instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=1) crown or a simple helmet with Jotun runes for a [giant instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=4) crown. When worn, the crown allows you to tap further into your instincts, granting you even greater benefits if the crown's essence matches your instinct. You must be able to [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=3) to use the crown's activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You roll initiative; **Requirements** You have [Quick-Tempered](/Actions.aspx?ID=2803) and meet its requirements;; **Effect** You use Quick-Tempered and gain 10 additional temporary Hit Points from your Rage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [rage](/Traits.aspx?ID=680)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re raging, and the crown’s instinct matches your barbarian instinct; **Effect** You draw upon your instinct to gain a boon, as follows. 

_Animal:_ Your bestial instincts take over, granting you greater ferocity. You can Stride as part of the activation, and you gain 10 additional temporary Hit Points from your rage. Until the end of your rage, you deal 3 additional damage with your animal’s unarmed attacks, and those unarmed attacks gain the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) d8 trait. 

_Dragon:_ The might of dragons surrounds you in the form of dancing flames, poisonous wisps, or another appropriate manifestation. Until the end of your rage, adjacent creatures that hit you with a melee attack, as well as creatures that touch you or hit you with an unarmed attack, take 2d6 damage each time they do. The damage type is that of your instinct’s dragon breath. 

_Fury:_ Your boundless fury allows you push past your natural limits, moving with unmatched speed. You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) until the end of your rage. You can use your extra action only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). 

_Giant:_ If you have the space to do so and aren’t already, you become Large. Your equipment grows with you but returns to its natural size if removed. Increase your reach by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you were Tiny). You deal 2 additional damage when using your larger weapon. Also, when you Stride along the ground, you can shatter the earth with your footfalls, and any squares you move through become [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). You ignore the difficult terrain you create. The ground reverts to normal when your rage ends. 

_Spirit:_ You take on the partial form of a spirit, becoming somewhat incorporeal. Your raging resistance applies to all physical damage and poison damage, although you halve this resistance against magical attacks. 

_Superstition:_ Your body fights off the effects of magic with even greater effect. Until the end of your rage, when you attempt a saving throw against magic, roll twice and take the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. Also, your raging resistance applies to all magic traditions while you rage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Instinct Crown

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 152 **Price** 160 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Repurposing and enchanting a helmet worn by a battlefield commander can create a _tactician’s helm_, imparting knowledge of battlefield tactics that feeds off your minor victories. The helm grants you a +1 item bonus to [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks. Also, a jewel adorns the brow of the helmet. This jewel becomes charged each time you hit a creature with a [Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256). A _tactician’s helm_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))**Cost** 1 charge from the helm; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You choose one of the following effects.
  • **Charge!** Stride twice.
  • **Move It!** You gain a +2 status bonus to Acrobatics and Athletics checks until the end of this turn.
  • **Protect!** If you're wielding a shield, Stride to a space adjacent to an ally, then Raise your Shield.
  • **Re-Arm!** Interact up to three times. Each of these actions must be used to do something listed under [Interact](/Rules.aspx?ID=2151).
name: Tactician's Helm

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armory Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2340)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Several small charms shaped like weapons hang from an _armory bracelet_, which is often brass. The bracelet has one charm each for the groups [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3), [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [dart](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6), [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9), [pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10), [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11), [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12), [sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), and [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15). Rare versions of the armory bracelet include charms for [firearms](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull one charm from the bracelet. The charm transforms into a weapon of your choice from the charm's weapon group. If the weapon requires ammunition, it appears with a quiver or pouch with 20 pieces of ammunition for the weapon. The weapon is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ weapon of the type you chose. After 1 minute, the weapon transforms into a non-magical version and remains until your next daily preparations. At that point, the weapon and any remaining ammunition crumble to dust and all the charms reappear on the bracelet. The weapon and ammunition created with the charm are noticeably different from others and can't be sold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armory Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2340)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Several small charms shaped like weapons hang from an _armory bracelet_, which is often brass. The bracelet has one charm each for the groups [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3), [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [dart](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6), [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9), [pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10), [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11), [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12), [sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), and [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15). Rare versions of the armory bracelet include charms for [firearms](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull one charm from the bracelet. The charm transforms into a weapon of your choice from the charm's weapon group. If the weapon requires ammunition, it appears with a quiver or pouch with 20 pieces of ammunition for the weapon. The weapon is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ weapon of the type you chose. After 1 minute, the weapon transforms into a non-magical version and remains until your next daily preparations. At that point, the weapon and any remaining ammunition crumble to dust and all the charms reappear on the bracelet. The weapon and ammunition created with the charm are noticeably different from others and can't be sold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Corpseward Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2341)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 80

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _corpseward pendant_ is usually shaped like the skull of a humanoid or small animal.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You become [undetectable](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) to undead creatures for 10 minutes. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) can’t see, hear, or smell you, or detect you with sensory capabilities such as [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82). A creature can attempt a DC 18 Will saving throw to ignore this effect. If an undead has reason to believe that undetected opponents are present, it can still attempt to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) you. If you attempt to use a [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) spell to damage undead, touch or damage an undead creature, or attack any creature while warded in this manner, the pendant’s effects immediately end. An undead creature who observes you in this manner or one who succeeds at the Will save is immune to the _corpseward pendant_ for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Corpseward Pendant

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ethersight Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2342)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153, [Pathfinder #158: Sixty Feet Under](/Sources.aspx?ID=47) pg. 75

**Price** 325 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _ethersight ring_ is a glass band containing a swirling cloud of gray smoke. When you invest the ring, the smoke becomes as transparent as the glass encapsulating it, and you can see clearly into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) with a range of 60 feet. Whether or not you have invested the ring, you are visible to creatures in the Ethereal Plane within the same range while wearing it. Although you can see these creatures and they can see you, you can affect each other only with abilities that cross between the Ethereal Plane and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ethersight Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hunter's Arrowhead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2343)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153, [Pathfinder #145: Hellknight Hill](/Sources.aspx?ID=4) pg. 77

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _hunter's arrowhead_ is meant to be worn as a charm, such as a pendant, or carried in a pocket or quiver. The arrowhead is etched with images sacred to the elven god [Ketephys](/Deities.aspx?ID=110). While you wear or carry the arrowhead, it infuses you with great skill at hunting, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks and attack rolls against any creature you've currently designated as your prey with [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10). A _hunter's arrowhead_ is also a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of Ketephys.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would miss with an attack made with a [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3);  **Effect** You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your attack roll, possibly turning a miss into a hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Arrowhead

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Granted by Katapesh's Pactmasters to influential merchants, exceptional [Zephyr Guards](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=35), and favorite retainers, a _pactmaster's grace_ is a crystal-studded blue platinum ring that sharpens the wearer's urban instincts. While invested, the ring grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws while you are in an urban setting, and this increases to a +3 item bonus if you have legendary proficiency in Guild [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), Katapesh Lore, Mercantile Lore, or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14). You also gain a +3 item bonus to Mercantile Lore checks while wearing the ring, and you can attempt checks that require a proficiency rank of master in Society.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pactmaster's Grace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ring of Discretion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2345)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 154, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 80

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _ring of discretion_ magically conceals any armor and sheathed weapons you're wearing by either turning them [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) or creating the illusion of ordinary clothes. The ring doesn't change your appearance beyond concealing weapons and armor. As soon as you wield a weapon affected by the ring, the weapon becomes obvious to onlookers and is no longer affected until you sheathe the weapon for at least 1 minute. A creature can use the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to examine you and [disbelieve](/Rules.aspx?ID=267) this illusion (DC 15), and it can attempt to do so without using an action each time it hits you with an attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Discretion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritsight Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2346)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 154, [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 81

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The opal set in the intricately carved ivory _spiritsight ring_ eventually becomes translucent and tickles your finger whenever an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature is nearby. When in the presence of a nearby incorporeal creature, even if it's within a solid object, you eventually detect the creature, though you might not do so instantly, and you can't pinpoint the location. This acts as a [vague sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=413), like humans' sense of smell. An incorporeal creature trying to hide its presence from this sense attempts a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against your Perception DC to hide from your vague sense, as normal for attempting to foil [special senses](/Rules.aspx?ID=414). You gain a +2 item bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) incorporeal creatures within 30 feet of you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiritsight Ring

You recover 3d10+8 Hit Points and deal the same amount of bludgeoning damage to the swarm. Hit Point recovery is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect.

Originally designed by a sect of [Gozren](/Deities.aspx?ID=8) priests, _goz masks_ were designed to help navigate the area around the Eye of Abendego. The masks couldn't contend with the might of the storm, but people all around the Mwangi Expanse still use them. These masks are typically made of wood and sport round, exaggerated features.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) caused by fog, smoke, and other obscuring vapors for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Herd Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2349)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 154, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 73

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

All _herd masks_ are linked to at least one other _herd mask_ and are usually sold in sets of multiple masks. Linked _herd masks_ look like one another, with only the slightest of differences to tell them apart.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swap locations with another creature wearing a linked _herd mask_ within 100 feet. If you and the creature you swapped with are disguised as each other, other creatures gain an immediate Perception check against the lower of your or the other wearer's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DCs to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) each other. On a failure, they don't realize the swap occurred.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724). You can link a _herd mask_ you create to another _herd mask_ in your possession when you finish crafting it, which causes it to be linked to the chosen mask as well as all other _herd masks_ that are also linked to the chosen mask.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Herd Mask

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of Mercy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2350)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 154, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 74

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This porcelain or alabaster mask portrays an angelic visage of kindness and mercy. The mask grants a +1 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are about to roll a variable number of Hit Points you restore from an action with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) trait;  **Effect** Roll twice to determine the number of Hit Points you restore and take the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mask of Mercy

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 74 **Price** 475 gp **Usage** worn mask --- A _mask of the cursed eye_ is decorated with at least one wide, staring eye. The first time each day you’re targeted with a detection, prediction, revelation, or scrying spell by a creature on your plane that you can’t perceive, the creature targeting you must attempt a DC 24 Will save. This effect is automatic and does not require you to Activate the item. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is unaffected, but you know you’ve been targeted with a spell that has one of these traits.
**Failure** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute. You know you’ve been targeted with a spell that has one of these traits.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and dazzled for 10 minutes, and the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2236). You gain a brief mental glimpse of the triggering creature and learn its approximate distance and direction.
name: Mask of the Cursed Eye

---

Covered with thick armor and bearing a thicker horn, a _rhinoceros mask_ grants you increased momentum. If you Stride at least 10 feet, your next melee Strike before the end of your turn ignores the Hardness of objects with a Hardness of 5 or less. If the object has more than Hardness 5, the mask grants no benefit.

A _shapespeak mask_ is carved into the shape of an animal, usually tailored to a species of beast that the user favors. **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #158: Sixty Feet Under](/Sources.aspx?ID=47) pg. 76

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A patchwork of [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) flesh makes up a _skinsaw mask_, which is stitched together with black silk or wire. It is distinctive for its bulbous orange eye—crafted from a magical glass bauble—and wide row of teeth. When worn, the mask amplifies your ability to sense fear in other creatures. You know the value of the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition of any observed creature, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) frightened creatures. Whenever you deal precision damage to a frightened creature, you deal 1 additional precision damage. If you aren’t [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 while wearing a _skinsaw mask_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skinsaw Mask

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skittering Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2355)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 74

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _skittering mask_ is a hand-carved, wooden, full-head mask that sports several holes along each side of the face. The first time each day that you begin your turn [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and within 25 feet of an enemy, skittering metallic insect legs emerge from the holes in the mask and Step 5 feet away from the nearest enemy, dragging your body along with the mask. If more than one enemy is equidistant, the mask Steps away from one of them at random. The mask possesses no special senses and does not react to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) enemies, nor can it distinguish that a creature not acting openly hostile is an enemy.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Skittering Mask
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 74

**Bulk** 1

</row>

level="2"
right="Item 8"

>
Skittering Mask (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 74

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The mask Steps 15 feet instead of 5 feet, and for 1 minute, each time you begin your turn unconscious and within 25 feet of an enemy, the mask Steps 15 feet away from the nearest enemy again. An ally can signal the mask with a single action, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) traits. If the mask hears the signal, it attempts to move you towards that ally when it Steps away from the nearest enemy.
name: Skittering Mask

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tlil Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2356)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155, [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Colorful, beaded _tlil masks_ are commonly found on the distant continent of Arcadia, but trade between the two regions means that they can also be found in the Mwangi Expanse as curiosities. These masks usually bear floral patterns and attune your senses to plants of all varieties.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your vision up to 60 feet sees through small amounts of living plant matter as though it were transparent. While this effect is active, creatures can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you due to living plants, such as small trees, vines, and grass. This vision also prevents them from [Hiding](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) past you using only living plants for concealment or cover. Other than the inability to use the cover to Hide or Sneak, this ability doesn't prevent plants from providing [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) to creatures or blocking line of effect. It also doesn't allow you to see through dead plant matter, such as the wooden walls of a building, or thick plant matter, such as the walls of a dungeon built entirely inside an enormous living tree. The effect lasts for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 169 --- A durable material when properly treated, bone can replace wood and metal when creating armor, shields, and weapons. The statistics for bone are applicable to a variety of other sturdy animal-based products, such as chitin, horn, ivory, coral, and shell, which can also be classified as bone for the purposes of determining item statistics. Bone elements from certain beasts, like the horn of a [unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397) or the spikes of a [manticore](/Monsters.aspx?ID=294), may even yield magical properties when worked by a skilled crafter. ## Bone Objects

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 169 --- A durable material when properly treated, bone can replace wood and metal when creating armor, shields, and weapons. The statistics for bone are applicable to a variety of other sturdy animal-based products, such as chitin, horn, ivory, coral, and shell, which can also be classified as bone for the purposes of determining item statistics. Bone elements from certain beasts, like the horn of a [unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397) or the spikes of a [manticore](/Monsters.aspx?ID=294), may even yield magical properties when worked by a skilled crafter. ## Bone Objects

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 169 --- Stone was one of the earliest materials used to create tools, and crafters can still fashion it into a variety of implements, weapons, and even armor. Simple stone objects are made from common materials such as flint or basalt, while exquisite stone objects might be made from crystal, obsidian, or semi-precious gems. Particularly notable stone armors or weapons might even be crafted entirely from a giant emerald, ruby, or sapphire, though they can still use the base statistics presented below. Stone can replace the striking surface of any melee weapon as long as it has no complex moving parts, and ammunition can be crafted with it. Stone can replace metal components in [chain](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=1) and [composite](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=2) armor; only [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=5) armor that specifically describes itself as being made from stone can be crafted from this material ## Stone Objects

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Cayden's Tankard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2360)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Light Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ordinary-looking silver tankard functions as a _+4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [hopeful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1381) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29)_ when wielded as a weapon. Imbued with [Cayden Cailean's](/Deities.aspx?ID=4) courage, you are immune to [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects. Any liquid poured into the tankard transforms into a strong, alcoholic ambrosia that remains contained safely within until you drink it. Drinking the ambrosia Activates the tankard, with one of the following effects. If you aren't the one blessed to borrow the tankard, you are [drained 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while holding it, and its magic doesn't function for you.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** You are targeted or included in the area of a fear effect;  **Effect** Calmly swigging a drink on the battlefield turns your foe's attempt to frighten you against them. The fear effect is [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) for all targets, and the creature that created the effect must attempt a saving throw as if it alone were the original target of the effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You drink from the tankard, ending the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) conditions on yourself and creatures of your choice within 120 feet of you, as well as anything giving such targets a circumstance penalty to Speed. Any effect causing these conditions ends, and if the source of the effect is an item, that item can't produce the effect for 1 week, provided it is of a level lower than the tankard's. If a target needs to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) an effect imposing any of these conditions, it automatically does so on its next attempt. You can Activate this ability even if one of the listed conditions would normally prevent you from doing so (such as paralyzed).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You enhance yourself with a shard of Cayden's divine fortune and cast [_indestructibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=699).

**Destruction** If an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creature carries _Cayden’s Tankard_ into the Starstone Cathedral, drinks from it, and returns outside with it, the tankard shatters.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cayden's Tankard

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Coldstar Pistols](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2361)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dueling Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201) in this paired set is etched with flames along its barrel, while the other is etched with icicles. In these separate forms, one gun comprising the _Coldstar Pistols_ functions as a _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) dueling pistol_ and the other as a _+3 greater striking [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) dueling pistol_. When joined, the guns form a double-barreled weapon that functions as a _+4 major striking greater flaming greater frost dueling pistol_. The combined form has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 120 feet. In either form, the Coldstar Pistols have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=327), [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), and [fatal d10 traits](/Traits.aspx?ID=178). As star guns, the _Coldstar Pistols_ run on magic and don't use ammunition or black powder.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You switch the _Coldstar Pistols_ from one form to the other. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) with a +4 circumstance bonus.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Make two Strikes against one target, taking the highest of the two attack rolls and applying it to both attacks. Your multiple attack penalty increases only after these Strikes.

**Destruction** If Deft Onki's name and deeds ever fade from mortal memory entirely, the _Coldstar Pistols_ can be destroyed like a normal object.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coldstar Pistols

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 179 **Usage** worn --- At the center of this silver ring gleams an obsidian gem, its surface emblazoned with a blood-red rune of forgetting. While wearing a _forgotten signet_, you’re subjected to [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556) (+32 counteract bonus) and easily fade from others’ memory. Sapient creatures must attempt a DC 42 Will save each time you depart from their company or they forget you entirely. **Destruction** If a creature learns the true name of a _forgotten signet's_ wearer and makes it public knowledge, that _forgotten signet_ tarnishes and breaks in two. **Critical Success** The creature's memories of you are normal.
**Success** The creature remembers you and your interactions but has difficulty describing either to others. Each time they attempt to share information about these subjects with someone else, they relate only confusing or unclear details about you and this interaction.
**Failure** The creature remembers your existence but no details about you or your interactions. For example, they remember speaking with an individual of your most basic biological traits, such as a human woman, but not your name, appearance, or other specific features.
**Critical Failure** The creature retains no memories of you or this interaction.
name: Forgotten Signet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Ghosthand's Comet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2363)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 179

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The barrel of this long rifle is translucent in places, forming a swirled pattern along the metal, and its stock is formed of crimson wood. _Ghosthand's Comet_ is a _+4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [beast-bane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1041) [greater impactful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1062) advanced firearm_ with a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 300 feet. It deals 5d8 force damage and has the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409), and [fatal d12 traits](/Traits.aspx?ID=178). As a star gun, _Ghosthand's Comet_ runs on magic and doesn't use ammunition or black powder. The weapon is silent when fired.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You attempt a ranged Strike with _Ghosthand's Comet_;  **Effect** For the triggering Strike, _Ghosthand's Comet_ changes its damage type to your choice of acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** On your next attempt at a ranged Strike with _Ghosthand's Comet_, the shot phases through any material or magical obstacle, such as a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363), in its path, ignoring all [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). You must attempt the Strike by the end of your turn or this effect is lost.

**Destruction** If the [Grim Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=250) slays the wielder of _Ghosthand's Comet_, the Reaper's scythe, as it strikes the killing blow, is destined to slice the star gun in half.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghosthand's Comet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Perfected Robes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2364)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 179

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These unadorned white robes, fastened with simple brass pins in the shape of a human hand, can’t be soiled or blemished. While wearing perfected robes, you don’t need to eat, sleep, or drink, but you can if you choose to. The robes bless you with constant [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) (+32 counteract bonus). A creature who dons these robes without earning them is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 3, [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 3, and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 3 while wearing them, gaining the _truesight_ but otherwise unable to use the robes’ magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** If your next action is to attempt a d20 roll with which you have legendary proficiency, roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_avatar_](/Spells.aspx?ID=16), gaining the abilities for [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10).

**Destruction** If the wearer ever willingly turns from the path of self-perfection into corruption or overindulgence, their _perfected robes_ crumble to nothing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Perfected Robes

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 180 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Carved from a scale of the [kaiju](/Traits.aspx?ID=383) Igroon, this jagged shield refracts light around it in a shimmering haze. A _scale of Igroon_ (Hardness 20, HP 160, BT 80) recovers 4 Hit Points at the start of its wielder's turn. When you [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), you can use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with the scale of Igroon to block an attack or effect that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, or sonic damage as well as physical damage. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** You're in an area of [bright](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408); **Effect** You angle the shield to refract light. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +4 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) and can do so while [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27). This bonus ends if you Activate another ability or use the Shield Block reaction. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** You're in an area of bright light; **Effect** You angle the shield at a target within 60 feet, reflecting light into its eyes. It must attempt a DC 42 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) until its next turn begins.
**Failure** The target is blinded for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded for 2d4 hours. **Activate** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** You use Shield Block and prevent yourself from taking energy damage from a line, ray, or a direct attack, including a [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) spell; **Effect** You reflect the energy along a trajectory you choose. The effect travels only up to its remaining range, using its original parameters if it strikes other targets. **Destruction** If a deity, kaiju, spawn of a deity, titan, or being of similar power stomps on a _scale of Igroon_ while in absolute darkness, the shield is destroyed permanently.
name: Scale of Igroon
