<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 24"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Starfaring Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2366)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 180

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The swirling folds of a _starfaring cloak_ appear to contain the night sky, with the stars rotating hypnotically through its firmament shedding dim light to a range of 10 feet. While wearing the cloak, you gain a +10-foot item bonus to your Speed and a fly Speed equal to your Speed. You can survive comfortably without breathing, in the void of space, and in [severe or extreme cold or heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642). Also, you gain sustenance from starlight and sunlight, so if you're outdoors for an hour or more per day, you don't need to eat or drink. While wearing the cloak, you can navigate perfectly and unerringly by looking up at the sky.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) ,[fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You make an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw;  **Effect** Reroll the triggering roll and take the higher result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The cloak casts [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) at 4th rank (DC 42).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** The cloak casts [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) at 10th rank. If you name no destination, it teleports you to a random planet in a random location that's safe for you.

**Destruction** If the wearer of a _starfaring cloak_ is bound by [imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) for a century and a day, the cloak dissolves into light. This time is reduced to a year and a day if the wearer is placed in eternal slumber by _imprisonment_ and subjected to the [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell once per day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Starfaring Cloak

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 180 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 **Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20) --- This _+4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater brilliant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1043) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ binds the souls of powerful creatures it slays. The soul can't be returned to life by any means while imprisoned within the glaive and can be freed only by a deed of great benevolence or selflessness. While using the glaive as a weapon, whenever you reduce a sapient creature of 18th level or higher to 0 Hit Points or roll a critical success with a Strike against such a target, the creature must attempt a DC 50 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** No additional effect.
**Success** The glaive siphons the creature's essence, rendering it [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). If the creature dies from the Strike or while drained in this way, the creature's soul is bound in the glaive.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is drained 2.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature is drained 4. The glaive also has the following activations. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You Strike with the _Whisperer of Souls_; **Effect** You choose whether the Strike deals lethal or [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) damage. If the glaive has a reason for doing so, such as slaying a creature whose soul the glaive can absorb, it can defy your choice if you fail a DC 50 Will save. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You attempt an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25), or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26); **Effect** You listen to eldritch secrets the weapon whispers, gaining a +4 item bonus to the triggering check. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week; **Effect** Attempt an Occultism check as if you cast the [legend lore](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual about a subject. If you roll a success or critical success, you can repeat what the _Whisperer of Souls_ relates to you about the subject. On a critical failure, you and the glaive are drawn into a murmuring void of cold, where your mind is assaulted by strange visions for an entire week. At the end of this time, you reappear and must attempt a DC 50 Will save. **Success** When you return, you can retrain one of your skills into a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill about evil creatures or places, as if you had spent 1 week retraining.
**Failure** As success, but you are [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 week.
**Critical Failure** As success, but you become [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). If you were [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), you instead lose that trait. You must retrain one of your skills into a Lore about unholy creatures or places. You become obsessed with increasing your forbidden knowledge, turning to wicked forces that offer such secrets and compromising your morals further if you must. **Destruction** If the _Whisperer of Souls_ is used to slay [Shelyn's](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) [herald](/Traits.aspx?ID=330), it disintegrates, bringing about great evil in its wake.
name: Whisperer of Souls

[Ursine Avenger Hood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2368)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<column gap="tiny">

---

_Note from Nethys: This item is a constituent part of the [Ursine Avenger Hood](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=200) archetype using the [archetype artifact](/Rules.aspx?ID=1961) rules._

A gift passed from warrior to warrior, this cowl is made from a [cave bear's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=48) skull and fur. Once invested, the wearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58), which increases to +2 if the animal is a [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=13). The wearer of the hood feels a constant longing for the wilderness and wild places and takes a –1 item penalty to all [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) if they go more than 5 days inside a large town or city without spending at least 4 hours in the wilderness.

**Destruction** Soak the _Ursine Avenger Hood_ in the blood of a [linnorm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=69) that is at least 16th level and then burn it in a fire that uses only oak and chrysanthemum as fuel.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ursine Avenger Hood

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Gelid Shard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2369)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 184

---

_Note from Nethys: This item is a constituent part of the [Gelid Shard](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=201) archetype using the [archetype artifact](/Rules.aspx?ID=1961) rules._

This crystal shard shimmers with its own light even as it seems to draw the heat out of its surroundings. Plunging it into your heart grants you the tranquil lethality of deepest winter, but the more you draw upon its power, the colder your heart grows. Your frozen heart dulls your emotions. When you gain a bonus from an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect, that bonus is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 0. At 10th level, the bonus is reduced by 2. Your dulled emotions make it hard to relate to others; the DC of checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) a favor of other creatures, or to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) in such attempts, is increased by +2. You gain resistance to cold equal to your level and a +2 status bonus to all saves against emotion effects.

**Destruction** The _gelid shard_ must be left exposed in the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6) for a week and a day, then smashed with an [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) hammer by someone who felt true love for the shard's creator.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gelid Shard

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Elder Seed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2370)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 187

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An enormous, perfect, ripe seed from a tree important to a region's druids, an _elder seed_ exudes vitality. Plant life grows quickly and abundantly around it. Anyone who touches the seed understands the nature of its power. Swallowing it whole imparts its effects, but succeeding at the save against the boon causes the swallower to regurgitate the seed.

---

**Stage 1 (9 Days)** Your skin hardens, giving you a brownish-green tinge. You gain resistance to bludgeoning damage and piercing damage equal to your level, and you have [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) out to a range of 30 feet. Also, you can cast [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102), [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), 4th-rank [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294), and [_web_](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) each once per day as primal innate spells. The web is a dense mycelial net rather than spider silk.

---

**Stage 2 (1 Month)** You gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416) and fluency in [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26). However, your body feels stiffer, your mind slower to move to intense emotions, and you are prone to take your time making decisions. You become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), take a –1 status penalty to initiative rolls, and gain a weakness to fire damage equal to half your level, and you take the same amount of additional damage from [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1).

If you die at this stage or higher, your body sinks into the earth, forming rich soil. Over several days, a mature, full-sized tree of the seed's type grows where you fell. If plant growth is impossible, such as due to lack of sunlight, fungi grow there instead, extending mycelia through the earth until they reach a place the tree can grow. Then, the tree grows. An [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) can tend this tree to cause it to regrow one _elder seed_ after 3 years and 3 days.

---

**Stage 3 (1 Month)** You become more treelike and ponderous in body and mind, and when you cast the _enlarge_ innate spell from this boon, you can cast it at 4th rank. The clumsy value increases to 2 and the status penalty increases to –2. Whenever you roll initiative, you become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) automatically, slowed 2 if the d20 roll is 10 or lower, or slowed 3 if the d20 shows a 1. Your weakness to fire and vulnerability to axes equals your level.

---

**Stage 4** You die.

---

**Purging** You must seek out an arboreal, [druid](/Classes.aspx?ID=5), or nature-loving [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) that is your level or higher whom you have never met, and pledge your allegiance to that being. Doing so places you under a [_geas_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) to accomplish at least one meaningful service, usually a quest, for your new liege. While under the geas, you can't advance to stage 4. Once you complete your service, you regurgitate the _elder seed_, and its effects on you end.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elder Seed

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Heartblood Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2371)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 187

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn ring

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sealed inside the hidden compartment of a gold ring, heartblood has an unmistakable coppery taste, along with a thick mouth feel. A whiff of the concoction is pleasantly stimulating.

---

**Stage 1 (1 Week)** The first time you enter this stage from a particular dose of _heartblood_, you are affected as if by [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248). Each round this effect functions, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) affecting you with a bonus equal to your class DC – 10. _Heartblood_ also reduces physical signs of aging, altering your appearance to that of vigorous adulthood for your ancestry. (Non-adults who drink _heartblood_ change only in apparent vigor, not age.)

 You have [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=796) equal to half your level, as well as resistance to negative damage and poison damage equal to half your level. Also, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws (or any other defense) against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, disease, [paralysis](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), poison, and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Treat the outcome of any saving throw you roll against death, paralyze, or sleep effects as one step better.

---

**Stage 2 (1 Month)** You gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416), or you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) if your ancestry already has low-light vision. Reduce your current and maximum Hit Points by 2 × your level; you are [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). You develop a pallor and a dislike of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), especially sunlight. If suddenly exposed to bright light, you are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the end of your next turn. While in an area of sunlight, you remain dazzled until 1 round after the exposure ends. Normal food is unsatisfying, and you have urges to consume blood; when confronted with the opportunity to do so, you must succeed at a Will save to avoid it. Doing so once after each time you roll initiative is enough. Your normal coloration returns for a few hours after you drink blood.

 If you die at this stage or higher, your body becomes a bloody mist that sinks 6 feet into the ground. The third night thereafter, you rise from the earth as a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) with the alignment of the _heartblood's_ creator. You are under the control of the _heartblood's_ creator unless your level is higher than that creature's. The controlling vampire senses you and guides you to them via psychic link. Significant destruction of your body and circumstances that prevent the formation of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) stop this effect.

---

**Stage 3 (1 Month)** You gain darkvision and now have fast healing equal to your level. A death effect can't automatically kill you or increase your dying value. Reduce your current and maximum Hit Points by 4 × your level, rounded up, instead of 2 × your level; you are doomed 2. Your hunger for blood increases, so if you fail to drink a cup or so each day, you are considered to have gone without food that day. You are also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) while in sunlight, becoming slowed 2 after 1 minute. Your shadow is wispy, and your reflection in mirrors ghostly, which are apparent signs of vampirism to those who know such facts.

---

**Stage 4** You die.

---

**Purging** _Heartblood_ loses its potency if the vampire who created it is destroyed. However, a dose of _heartblood_ imposes a –2 status penalty to your saving throws against the powers of the vampire who created it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heartblood Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Phoenix Cinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2372)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 188

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** touched

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An incandescent, multipronged crystal the color of dying fire, a _phoenix cinder_ gives off pleasant warmth and a sense of peace. A low, soft chirruping around the crystal invokes the idea of using fire to cleanse and protect. The crystal offers this power to anyone who touches it. A partaker must be willing to gain the boon's effects.

---

**Stage 1 (1 Week)** The first time you enter this stage from a particular _phoenix cinder_, you are affected as if by [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248).

 For 3 days after taking in the cinder, you are immune to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72). While in this stage or higher, you have [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=796) equal to half your level, as well as resistance to fire damage equal to half your level. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws (or any other defense) against fire. Treat the outcome of any saving throw you roll against fire effects as one step better. You can cast [_produce flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) as an innate spell, and you have access to the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Frequency** once  per day;   **Effect** You wreathe yourself in flame for 1 minute. Adjacent creatures that hit you with a melee attack, as well as creatures that touch you or hit you with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack, take 2d6 fire damage. You can Dismiss the activation.

---

**Stage 2 (1 Month)** You can use the activation once per hour. Always feverish and flushed, you feel as if temperatures lower than sweltering are cold. Reduce your current and maximum Hit Points by 2 × your level, and you have a weakness to cold damage equal to half your level. You have urges to burn and use fire when you can. When confronted with the opportunity to do so, you must succeed at a Will save to avoid it. Doing so once after each time you roll initiative is enough.

 If you die at this stage or higher, your body burns from the inside out over 1d4 rounds, reducing you to fine ash. Anything flammable on you is destroyed. A [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137) emerges from your remains, but doesn’t have the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait and is interested more in selfish pursuits and spreading fire than kindness. You also attempt a DC 8 flat check; on a failure, the phoenix gains the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait, and also thirsts for vengeance against the little beings like the one who dared to use its power for a time. 

---

**Stage 3 (1 Month)** You now have fast healing equal to your level but reduce your current and maximum Hit Points by 4 × your level, rounded up, instead of 2 × your level. Also, you have a weakness to cold damage equal to your level and a weakness to evil damage equal to half your level. You can use the activation you previously gained once per minute, but you can no longer Dismiss it. Whenever you drop to 0 Hit Points, a [_flame strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=120) erupts from a randomly chosen point on the edge of your space.

---

**Stage 4** You die.

---

**Purging** You must collect the remains of a phoenix of your level or higher who has been unable to self-resurrect, compressing the remains with powdered diamonds, opals, and rubies worth 75 gp × your level into an egg. You can then use _flame strike_ as if you dropped to 0 Hit Points during this boon's stage 3. If you do so, the phoenix whose remains you collected is reborn as if it used its Self-Resurrection, and the _phoenix cinder's_ effects end on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phoenix Cinder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Void Fragment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2373)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 189

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** touched

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Hovering midair, thrumming and whispering of its power, a _void fragment_ is a crystalline fragment of swirling colors, reminiscent of stars and deep darkness. It thrums intermittently with a beckoning vibration, drawing attention from and pulling at nearby creatures with a tangible sense of gravity. A chill hangs in the air around it. At a touch, the wisp vanishes, giving the point of contact a gangrenous color. If the partaker succeeds at the initial save, the wisp reappears somewhere on the same planet.

---

**Stage 1 (8 Days)** The first time you enter this stage from a given void fragment, you gain the effect of a [_potion of truespeech_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2954) for 3 days. The wisp grants you [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) with a range of 30 feet. Also, you are under the effects of [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), except that its status bonus is +1 and it [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) with a bonus equal to your class DC – 9. The wisp grants you resistance to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) damage equal to half your level. You also have access to the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. If you used 2 actions, you can teleport up to 60 feet.

---

**Stage 2 (24 Days)** You gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) thoughtsense to half your telepathy's range, which increases to 60 feet. However, you have a tough time filtering the mental noise. If any creatures can be detected by your thoughtsense, you take a –1 penalty to Perception checks. When you arrive in a space after using the activation to teleport, you create a 10-foot emanation that works like [_strange geometry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=719)  for 1 round. This emanation can affect you.

 If you die at this stage or higher, your body implodes. Your soul reincarnates as an [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1) of a level close to yours, which extrudes from the location of your implosion. Common forms include [jah-tohl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2930), [gosreg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2931), [quelaunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3159), [rhu-chalik](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2928), [shoggoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=368), and [xoarian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2929). This creature isn't you, but fleeting memories of your former existence remain with it. You can't be raised from the dead while this monster endures. The _void fragment_ reappears somewhere on the same planet.

---

**Stage 3 (32 Days)** Your telepathy range increases to 100 feet, and the wisp grants you resistance to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) damage equal to your level. The mental noise increases the penalty to Perception to –2. Whenever you roll initiative, you become confused for up to 1d4 rounds.

---

**Stage 4** You die.

---

 **Purging** You must find and slay a powerful [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) or a servant of such creatures the GM deems appropriate. The creature must be of your level or higher. Then, someone must cast a [_rest eternal_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=153) ritual on you and the dead being; you must be anointed with the same oils the ritual normally requires. If the ritual results in a success, it casts the slain creature into the void along with the _void fragment_ inside you, ending its effects on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Void Fragment

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bellicose Dagger](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2374)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The hilt of a _bellicose dagger_ is etched with the word for “war” in various languages. Having absorbed the spirit of violence over the course of its existence, the weapon now craves bloodshed. A _bellicose dagger_ is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_. However, anytime you interact with creatures, no matter the context, you must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or else find you have, as a reaction, drawn the dagger.

 When you use the _bellicose dagger_ as a weapon for the first time, it fuses to you. While it's in your possession, you take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks and skill checks to avoid fighting, such as [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to trick your way out of a scuffle or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to sneak past enemies.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bellicose Dagger

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bower Fruit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2375)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Bower fruit_ got its name from its association with [the Green Mother](/Deities.aspx?ID=93), a fey Eldest with a fondness for plants and manipulation, whose domain is known as the Hanging Bower. She uses these cursed fruits to keep mortals in her thrall, but it's unknown whether she created them or simply popularized their use.

 A _bower fruit_ condemns a non-fey who tastes it to never again be satisfied with the fare found in the mundane [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Any fruit can be cursed in this manner, but stone fruits such as peaches, plums, mangoes, and lychees are most common. Upon activating a bower fruit by eating it, you’re afflicted with the curse of decadence. You can’t recover from stage 1 of the curse naturally, even if you succeed at the save to do so. 

**Curse of Decadence** (curse) **Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Stage 1** You’re afflicted by hunger as if you hadn’t eaten food in days. You become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) and take 1d4 damage each day that can’t be healed until you sate your hunger. No amount of eating food can sate your hunger during the spell’s duration, with one exception—food from the First World sates your hunger and suppresses the effects, but permanently increases the save DC by 1 (to a maximum of DC 35). After the week ends, if your hunger is still not sated, you take damage from [starvation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603). (1 week); **Stage 2** As stage 1, plus you’re [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1; **Stage 3** As stage 1, plus you’re [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and sickened 1; **Stage 4** As stage 1, plus you’re drained 2, sickened 2, and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bower Fruit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Corruption Cassock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2376)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 190

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +20; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), four other languages)

**Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19

**Int** +2, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +4

**Will** +20 

</column>

---        

Devotees of benevolent faiths fear and loathe corruption cassocks, each of which is dedicated to an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) deity. When the garment chooses a target, such as when it senses a worshipper of a deity who isn’t unholy, it alters its form to match another deity’s iconography, appearing to be a devoted vestments for that deity. Once donned, the cassock tries to subtly sway its wearer to the worship of its deity. If the wearer begins to succumb, the _corruption cassock_ gradually reverts to its true appearance, replacing its false iconography as its wearer converts.

A _corruption cassock_ functions as a [_devoted vestments_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077) for any wearer it chooses. Once you invest the cassock, it fuses to you. While you remain loyal to another deity, you believe the cassock grants you a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks and a +1 item bonus to the divine skill of the deity to whom you believe the cassock is dedicated. It does neither. The GM secretly adjusts your checks with those skills, causing you to fail at religious tasks more often. If you have multiple equal or lower item bonuses to the affected skills, the cassock takes precedence, negating those bonuses. If you convert to following the cassock’s true unholy deity, however, these bonuses function normally for you.

If you attempt to Cast a Spell that has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait, the cassock can force you to attempt a DC 28 Will save. On a failure, you’re [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for one minute (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). A _corruption cassock_ uses this ability to convince you that your deity has forsaken you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a  cleric domain spell for a domain belonging to the deity you believe the cassock is dedicated to. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost. However, when you use this activation, the _corruption cassock_ can instead cast a domain spell of the same rank from its deity. It does so only to confuse and dismay you, harming your faith.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Corruption Cassock

>
[Crushing Coils](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2377)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from constrictor snakeskin, the strips of this _+1 [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ wrap around you like an anaconda might wrap around its victim. The first time you roll a 1 on any attack roll or check after donning the armor, it fuses with you and constricts. It constricts anytime you roll a 1 on any attack roll or check thereafter. When the armor constricts, you're [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) for 1 round.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The armor wraps around you, allowing you to don it by the time the activation finishes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crushing Coils

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Energy Toxin Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2378)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=672" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glass bottle embellished with brass decorations appears to be a [_lesser energy breath potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2941) of a specific type, but has been polluted, making it toxic to use. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You drink from the bottle and must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude saving throw or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure) and immediately vomit the potion directly onto yourself, taking 5d6 damage of the same damage type the potion deals and expending the item. If you succeed at your Fortitude save, you become sickened 1 but otherwise are able to unleash Energy Breath as normal for a _lesser energy breath potion_ of the appropriate type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energy Toxin Bottle

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 191 --- The origins of the notorious _Dullahan Codex_ are shrouded in mystery. Some legends claim it belongs to a [dullahan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=171) whose head was taken by the [Grim Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=250). Others attribute its creation to a powerful necromancer whose name has been lost to time. Whatever the truth, the grimoire has passed down through the ages, sometimes via mortal hands and other times mysteriously appearing among the possessions of its next target. It deserves its reputation for dooming those who possess it to die, but scholars debate whether the codex causes this fate or merely acts as its harbinger. The _Dullahan Codex_ is a jet-black tome bearing a single rune embossed on its cover, and it functions as a _[true endless grimoire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=992)_. Inside, scrawled across its parchment pages in a delicate, spidery script, is a lengthy list of names that always appears in a reader's native alphabet. The grimoire isn't sentient, but it selects its owners, quickly passing out of the hands of those it doesn't choose. An intended victim's name appears on the list of names. If your name is on the list and you touch the volume or read from its pages, you must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Critical Success** The codex disappears, moving on to a new victim.
**Success** The codex fuses to you.
**Failure** The codex fuses to you, and you become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but you're doomed 2. If you attempt to get rid of the codex while it's fused to you, it returns to your possession within an hour. Each day the codex is fused to you, you must attempt another Will saving throw, but a critical success does nothing. The doomed value from the codex can decrease only after it's no longer fused to you; once it's fused to you, you remain its intended victim unless you complete a [_freedom_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=11) ritual aimed at ending this 10th-rank effect. (On a critical failure with this ritual, the codex adds all casters to its list.) You can redirect the curse by inscribing another person's name in the grimoire and succeeding at a DC 40 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check. Doing so is an evil act. If the curse is ritually ended or redirected, or the chosen victim dies, the codex moves on to a new victim.
name: Dullahan Codex
search_markdown:
<traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Gaffe Glasses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2380)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These wire-rim glasses appear to be [_glasses of sociability_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1057), making you think they grant a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) when they grant none. The GM secretly adjusts your Diplomacy checks to ignore the bonus, and if you have multiple +1 item bonuses to Diplomacy, the glasses take precedence, negating those bonuses.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** Like _glasses of sociability_, with the same limitations, you stare at another creature. If you've met and exchanged names, you expect to instantly remember the target's name. However, you recall the worst possible incorrect name, such as mistaking a famous artist for their hated rival. This blunder doesn't prevent you from realizing the creature's real name after you've been corrected. Once you use this activation, the glasses fuse to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gaffe Glasses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Handkerchief of Disagreeable Disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2381)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant scarf appears to be and functions as a [_masquerade scarf_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3095). When you invest the scarf, it fuses to you.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Like a _masquerade scarf_, the scarf casts a 1st-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) spell on you. However, the illusion disadvantages you based on your intent, making you, for example, appear to be a suspicious ruffian if you’re trying to sneak past guards or lending you the seeming of a pauper if you’re trying to impress a shallow aristocrat. You and those you consider to be allies must succeed at a DC 16 Will save or you perceive the [illusion](/Rules.aspx?ID=267) as you intended it, though others won’t. Evidence to the contrary allows you to attempt to disbelieve the false version of the illusion. You can’t Dismiss the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Heckling Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2382)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk**  as tools of the normal type
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +9; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (two common languages)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, one skill associated with their use +9

**Int** +2, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +2

**Will** +7 

</column>

---        

Tools that are severely misused or left in malevolent circumstances can develop malicious sapience, dedicated to critiquing those who use them. Such _heckling tools_ are often born from implements useful to adventurers because such people are the likely to misuse tools or leave them in a corrupting situation. When you first set to using the tools, they fuse to you. Used for their intended purpose, the tools telepathically badger and disparage you, mocking your abilities and giving you ill-founded advice. You must succeed at a DC 19 Will save to realize this badgering comes from the tools and not your own negative thoughts. Instead of the tool's usual bonus, you take a –2 circumstance penalty to checks made using _heckling tools_. Once you realize the tools are cursed, you can suppress their negative effects, gaining their typical bonus for 24 hours if you succeed at a DC 17 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check to placate them, often by offering obsequious, public admiration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heckling Tools

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Luckless Dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2383)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved of bone, _luckless dice_ appear to be a set of [loaded dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1333). If unsuccessfully identified as cursed, _luckless dice_ seem to hold a minor enchantment that improves your luck. However, they fuse to you when you use them, cursing you with ill fortune. _Luckless dice_ don't grant a bonus on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks. Instead, when you use them to gamble, the GM rolls secretly, granting you the lower result. Once you realize the dice are cursed, the GM can instead allow you to roll twice and take the lower result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Luckless Dice

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Mother Maw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2384)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** placed on a surface

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item appears to be and functions as a [_portable hole_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2195), but it's actually the maw of an alien extradimensional creature akin to and older than a [_bag of devouring_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=598). Any animal or vegetable matter put in the hole has a chance of triggering the creature's interest. Whenever you reach into the hole to retrieve an item or place an animal or plant product within the bag, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, the hole ignores the intrusion. On a failure, the _mother maw_ devours the triggering material, removing it from existence. The maw can't eat [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253). If the triggering material isn't entirely inside the maw, such as when someone reaches inside, the _mother maw_ attempts to pull it completely inside using a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) action with a +28 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) bonus. On a success, it devours the victim or object.

 Whenever the maw critically fails the Athletics check, it regurgitates one creature or object it previously devoured. The condition of the vomited creature or object depends on its resilience and the time it has spent inside the maw. Typically, the maw spews forth items it finds difficult to digest, such as those made of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) or protected by magic. It can and does regurgitate remains, though.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mother Maw

Carved of acacia wood, a _ring of ravenousness_ is inlaid with a geometric pattern. The ring appears to be a _[ring of sustenance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=459)_. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Shared-Pain Sankeit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2386)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=34)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _shared-pain sankeit_ seems to be an impressive suit of _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2918) [sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=34)_ shaped to resemble a mighty [linnorm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=69). However, it protects you by drawing on the health of your nearby allies. When you roll the flat check for the armor's _fortification_ rune, it protects you normally when you roll a 20. When you roll a 17, 18, or 19, though, the GM determines damage for the critical hit normally, then distributes half to you and the other half evenly among allies within 30 feet of you. If no ally is within range to take the distributed damage, the _fortification_ rune fails to function. This effect manifests the first time an attacker scores a critical hit on you, after which the armor fuses with you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shared-Pain Sankeit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Shrieking Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2387)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A skull-topped metal shrieking key appears to be a [_skeleton key_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=266), useful in place of [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58) when attempting to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70). A _shrieking key_ has no activation, however. When you use it to Pick a Lock, the key emits a loud shriek audible for 500 feet despite ambient noise. Physical barriers still block or muffle the shriek as normal. You also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check rather than enjoying a bonus. After you attempt such a check with the key the first time, it fuses to you, returning to your possession if discarded. To use another device to Pick a Lock, you must first succeed at a DC 20 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shrieking Key

>
[Sluggish Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2388)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A silver charm bracelet, a _sluggish bracelet_ appears to be a [_bracelet of dashing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=415), granting you a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks. If the curse goes unrecognized, you think you can Activate it to gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed for 1 minute. Instead, its activation is as follows.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You take a –10-foot penalty to your Speed for 1 minute, and the bracelet fuses to you. Thereafter, it grants you no bonus to Acrobatics checks, and it imposes a –5-foot status penalty to your Speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sluggish Bracelet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Spurned Lute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2389)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of a deep-brown rosewood, a _spurned lute_ is adorned with carved flowers. The lute appears to be and functions as a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37). (Other _spurned instruments_ exist, but the lute is the least rare.) This lute has a jealous streak, demanding total loyalty from its “partner” musician. After you play the lute for the first time, it fuses to you. If you go a day without using it to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59), you become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) until you next do so. After that, when you attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check using an instrument other than the lute, you take a –4 circumstance penalty to do so, and you must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become stupefied 1 for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spurned Lute

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Tattletale Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2390)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _tattletale orb_ is a polished crystal sphere that appears to function as a [_crystal ball_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=253). If those whom you use the orb to scry on roll better than a critical failure on their saving throw, they receive a telepathic message alerting them to the scrying. A success or better at the save allows the target to choose to allow you to scry anyway, knowing they can use an aspect of the orb against you, according to the orb's type. A creature that rolls a critical success on the saving throw also learns your name and location. Once you Activate a _tattletale orb_ or use it to cast one of your scrying spells, it fuses to you. You must succeed at a Will save, using the scrying Will DC of a _crystal ball_ of the orb's type, to use another such device.

 _Tattletale orbs_ come in the same types as _crystal balls_, with the same activations and powers. However, your target must roll a critical failure on the saving throw for the orb to function as normal for that type of _crystal ball_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Tattletale Orb (Clear Quartz)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Tattletale Orb (Selenite)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb’s [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) for 24 hours and can choose to be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to the orb’s scrying during that time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Tattletale Orb (Moonstone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb's [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) for 24 hours and can use _mind reading_ against you instead, as if the target used the orb.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Tattletale Orb (Peridot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The target can send you one [_telepathic demand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=336).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Tattletale Orb (Obsidian)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb’s [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) for 24 hours. During that time, you see a false vision of the target’s choice if you scry on the target. Despite being higher rank than the spell, you can’t disbelieve this illusion if scrying through the orb.
name: Tattletale Orb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Tattletale Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2390)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _tattletale orb_ is a polished crystal sphere that appears to function as a [_crystal ball_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=253). If those whom you use the orb to scry on roll better than a critical failure on their saving throw, they receive a telepathic message alerting them to the scrying. A success or better at the save allows the target to choose to allow you to scry anyway, knowing they can use an aspect of the orb against you, according to the orb's type. A creature that rolls a critical success on the saving throw also learns your name and location. Once you Activate a _tattletale orb_ or use it to cast one of your scrying spells, it fuses to you. You must succeed at a Will save, using the scrying Will DC of a _crystal ball_ of the orb's type, to use another such device.

 _Tattletale orbs_ come in the same types as _crystal balls_, with the same activations and powers. However, your target must roll a critical failure on the saving throw for the orb to function as normal for that type of _crystal ball_.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Unfathomable Stargazer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2391)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _unfathomable stargazer_ is a handheld brass telescope etched with constellations, the patterns of which form bizarre, shifting runes. You can observe the night sky with an _unfathomable stargazer_, however, the constellations you glimpse through the telescope distort with expanding and contracting fields of darkness among them. You use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) instead of Perception or [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) when observing the skies with this item, and gain a +2 item bonus on all checks to do so. Once you use it, it fuses to you, and you must succeed at a DC 35 Will save to use another device to observe the stars, including your naked eyes. The telescope imposes a –4 circumstance penalty to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) or navigate. If you critically fail this Survival check, you're subjected to [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (DC 34) as you glimpse something horrifying and alien amid the darkness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unfathomable Stargazer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Arboreal Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2392)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +25; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision (low-light) 60 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet, imprecise [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) 30 feet (only when touching the ground)

**Communication** speech ([Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +25

**Int** +4, **Wis** +6, **Cha** +4

**Will** +27 

</column>

---        

Rather than passing on when they feel their death approaching, a rare few elderly [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) undergo a ritual of preservation. This rite fuses the arboreal's consciousness into a branch, allowing their spirit to live on within the object. The branch functions as a [_greater verdant staff_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=364), though the arboreal spirit allows only partners willing to safeguard nature to use the staff's spells. An arboreal who decides to become an _arboreal staff_ is more connected to shorter-lived species than others of their kind. Most choose to endure to share their knowledge with future generations, but they take a long view in ways that can be frustrating to their wielders.

 In addition to the activation and spells of a _greater verdant staff_, an _arboreal staff_ can cast the spells it holds as if it were the staff's wielder, though it usually does so only to benefit a partner. The staff also has [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) available as a 3rd-rank spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arboreal Staff

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bleachguard Doll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2393)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 194

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +16; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision ([low-light](/Rules.aspx?ID=416)) 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech (one language spoken by the doll's creator)

**Skills** One Intelligence-, Wisdom-, or Charisma-based skill chosen by the creator at +16

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +16 

</column>

---        

Rather than seeking out new experiences to stave off the Bleaching, [gnome](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3) spellcasters with the right know-how can infuse a small fragment of their consciousness into a doll, then send the doll off with a crew of adventurers to experience new things. Such gnomes might have difficulties that keep them from exploring or might be preoccupied with other pursuits, but the doll acts as an intermediary, transmitting the benefits of new experiences to its creator via a potent, but passive, psychic link. A _bleachguard doll_ has the alignment and demeanor of its creator, but new experiences can cause the two to diverge. The doll's creator is neither able to control it nor are they constantly aware of its activities. If the original creator perishes, the doll sometimes becomes inert (essentially dying as well), sometimes remains an intelligent item, and sometimes becomes a normal [soulbound doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=383), diminished in power but with some of the item's capabilities.

 Once per day, the doll sends its creator a dream of the day’s events. Each _bleachguard doll_ can cast the cantrips [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) (3rd rank), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), and one other 3rd-rank cantrip, along with one spell (at 3rd rank) that can be cast once per day. The creator chooses the cantrip and spells, which can’t require an attack roll or saving throw, during the doll’s creation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The _bleachguard doll_ casts one of its available cantrips or spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _bleachguard doll_ animates per [_summon construct_](/Spells.aspx?ID=318) as a soulbound doll, retaining pertinent item statistics, cantrips, and its spell instead of those of the soulbound doll. The _soulbound doll_ can Cast a Spell only if the bleachguard doll has a use of that spell remaining for the day. When reduced to 0 Hit Points or this activation ends, the _bleachguard doll_ becomes inert but repairs itself at the rate of 1 Hit Point per hour. This activation is unavailable until the doll has 23 Hit Points again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bleachguard Doll

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 194

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +20; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), and two other common languages)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19, [Sarenrae Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23

**Int** +2, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +4

**Will** +20 

</column>

---        

Prayer beads given prolonged exposure to spiritual energies at sacred [Sarenite](/Deities.aspx?ID=292) sites can attain sapience as _Dawnflower beads_. Other such objects hold the spirits of Sarenite priests who dedicated themselves to their work beyond death. Dawnflower beads function as a [_greater shining symbol_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3109). They don’t re-attune to other deities but allow any divine spellcaster who isn’t [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) to use them, though they attempt to talk their wielder out of morally questionable acts. Dawnflower beads have the following additional activations.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;     **Effect** The beads cast 4th-rank [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The beads cast 5th-rank [_vital luminance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1837).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dawnflower Beads

**Perception** +23; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision ([darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)) 60 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25

**Will** +25 

</column>

---        

A suit of [_autumn's embrace_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1840) armor can gain sapience when lovingly crafted by a fae monarch of sufficient power; a suit of _faerie queen's bower_ is one such example. The armor is happy to give you and your companions counsel, hoping to guide you on a path of benevolence and aid you in battles against forces that seek to cause harm to the natural world. It refuses access to its magic to anyone who causes undue harm to plants or animals.

 In addition to the features and activation of _autumn's embrace_, _faerie queen's bower_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts 4th-rank [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=712), with the protected area beginning from your square and extending to a place of relative safety.

</column>

>
[Inquisitive Quill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2396)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +11; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech (five languages)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +10

**Int** +4, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +1

**Will** +11 

</column>

---        

A colorful feather adorns an _inquisitive quill_, which never runs out of ink. Essentially a Tiny construct, an _inquisitive quill_ can stand on its own when given a surface, balancing as it writes or stands. (Drawing a line gives it an effective Speed of 5 feet.) It has strong curiosity, eagerly writing any information offered to it, often acting as a scribe for its partner. The quill copies text and drawings it can see or transcribes dictation it hears. It writes at a rate that rivals that of an expert scribe.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Kindled Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2397)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 195

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +17; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet, imprecise sense of heat 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), and two other common languages)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +21, [Fire Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +17

**Int** +4, **Wis** +0, **Cha** +5

**Will** +17 

</column>

---        

A _kindled tome_ is a [_book of lingering blazes_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=990) awakened to sapience and imbued with an enthusiasm for fire. It encourages you to learn new [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) spells to inscribe within its pages. When you attempt a skill check to [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25) to add a spell with the fire trait to the _kindled tome_, you treat your result as one step better than you rolled. The tome can be disparaging about other spells as “a waste of good page space,” especially if those spells have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) traits. In addition to the activation of a _book of lingering blazes_, a _kindled tome_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You Cast a Spell with the fire trait;  **Effect** The tome makes the flames linger, dealing 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to all you hit with or who fail the saving throw against the effect (doubling on a critical success on a spell attack and on a critical failure on a saving throw).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tome casts [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) at 5th level to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kindled Tome

[Majordomo Torc](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2398)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 195

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +13; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and 1 imparted language)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +13, [Nobility Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +14 

</column>

---        

Forged in platinum, a _majordomo torc_ is engraved with heraldic insignias along with one language's alphabet, much like a [_choker of elocution_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=422). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the torc's language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the flat check to perform an auditory action while [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) from 5 to 3.

 Upon being invested, the torc appoints itself your majordomo and, given the chance, takes over coordinating your social calendar, engagements, and wardrobe. A _majordomo torc_ has a prim, fussy disposition, and although it defers to you, it can grow sardonic if you frequently ignore its advice. The torc has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _majordomo torc_ casts [_befitting attire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=867) on you, usually to your specifications. However the torc can also choose the appearance of the illusion for you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Pathfinder's Mentor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2399)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 196

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +15; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and two other common languages)

**Skills** [Pathfinder Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +1

**Will** +14 

</column>

---        

A _Pathfinder’s mentor_ is a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3117) that has developed sapience. It has two aeon stone slots, and both stones grant their resonant powers without interfering with each other. If both slots are filled, the wayfinder and two stones count as two items toward your investiture limit. Most _Pathfinder’s mentors_ are eager explorers that object to staying in one location for long, urging their bearers toward ancient ruins to explore, relics to study, and discoveries to make. To this end, the _wayfinder_ acts as a guide, making [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks on your behalf during your travels. A Pathfinder’s mentor also has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The _wayfinder_ casts [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _wayfinder_ casts [_wanderer's guide_](/Spells.aspx?ID=368) on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pathfinder's Mentor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Phantom Piano](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2400)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 196

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** 2 hands

**Bulk** 16
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +22; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (any languages the possessing spirit knew in life)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +23, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21

**Int** +3, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +5

**Will** +22 

</column>

---        

When a skilled musician dies before reaching their full potential or accomplishing an important goal, their soul might refuse to move on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). Rather than becoming [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), the spirit possesses an instrument they played in life, turning it into an intelligent item and a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37). (Other _phantom instruments_ exist, but most are heavy.) A _phantom piano_ offers a bargain to any bard or other skilled musician who finds it: receive musical aid as you dedicate yourself to reaching the success the spirit felt they were denied in life. The piano refuses to play for those who reject this deal. If you accept, the piano asks only that you practice with it and perform for increasingly refined and august audiences, requiring a successful [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check appropriate to your proficiency level. The piano sets no strict timeline, but at the GM's discretion, if you go too long without making progress, the piano can cease cooperating with you until it's satisfied with your dedication once again.

 While you keep up your end of the bargain, the piano grants you a +2 item bonus to Performance checks made with it. The piano also provides access to composition spells based on your Performance proficiency rank, as if you had the corresponding feat. The spells you can use comprise a cumulative list, but you can cast them only when using the piano. You must spend the actions and Focus Points required to cast these spells.

**Trained**  [_courageous anthem_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [_uplifting overture_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1779)

**Expert**  [hymn of healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=4574), [_rallying anthem_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1773)

**Master**  [_symphony of the unfettered heart_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1777)

**Legendary**  [_soothing ballad_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1776)

 A _phantom piano_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The piano shrinks down to a figurine of light Bulk compact enough to store in a pouch, or from figurine form back to instrument form. In figurine form, the piano doesn't function as an instrument or provide magic other than this activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to Cast a Spell the piano provides you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phantom Piano
