</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 196

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +30; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and two other languages, usually regional and ancestral)

**Skills** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +31, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +31

**Int** +5, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +5

**Will** +30 

</column>

---        

Each _purloining cloak_ is a [_mercurial mantle_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1067) once worn by a legendary thief or two. The cloak became infused with a daredevil spirit and a penchant for high-stakes thievery. If its owner isn't willing to partake in such activities, the cloak tries to convince them to sell or give it to someone who will. A purloining cloak can use tendrils of its cloth to attempt [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68), proudly offering you its loot later. In addition to those of a _mercurial mantle_, a _purloining cloak_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The cloak casts 2nd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) on you, usually to your specifications. However the cloak can choose the appearance of the illusion for you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The cloak assesses the price of valuables it can see. This valuation doesn't include the value of features the cloak can't discern, such as magical properties.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Purloining Cloak

**Int** +4, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +0

**Will** +12 

</column>

---        

Sacred to the faith of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=33), a _toolkit of bronze whispers_ has been used with such devotion it has developed a consciousness and personality that can be a blessing to a partnered crafter. A _toolkit of bronze whispers_ functions as a set of [sterling artisan's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3). Such toolkits focus on new creations and enthusiastically suggest crafting techniques to wielders. A _toolkit of bronze whispers_ knows common item formulas of its level or lower and any other formulas the GM chooses. It can teach these formulas. You must still spend the time to copy a formula before you can use it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Toolkit of Bronze Whispers

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 197

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +30; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision ([darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415)) 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and two other common languages; [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +32

**Int** +6, **Wis** +10, **Cha** +5

**Will** +32 

</column>

---        

An encounter with a [toshigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1204), the enigmatic [kami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=243) who protect cherry trees, is rare, though often sought after and treasured by those who achieve such an encounter. Only a handful of mortals can truthfully claim to have seen a toshigami, let alone met one, though many popular fireside tales tell of virtuous souls receiving a toshigami's blessing to fight for a worthy cause. Such stories have a basis in fact; every so often, a toshigami gives a worthy mortal a flower from their ward, time-locked in perfect bloom and granted sapience. Such _toshigami blossoms_ are more sociable than their creators. Like toshigami, a blossom has a strong curiosity about the mortal world. If you wear a _toshigami blossom_, it can intercede for you, helping you make a good impression.

 A _toshigami blossom_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The blossom casts [_nature’s pathway_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) on you to your specifications. If you teleport through only cherry trees, the spell is cast at 6th rank.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The blossom casts [_soothing blossoms_](/Spells.aspx?ID=999) to your specifications.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The blossom sends a flurry of cherry blossoms outward in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) that lasts 1 round. You and your allies can see through these blossoms. To all other creatures, creatures within the cloud of blossoms become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. When you or an ally succeeds with a Strike against a creature in the blossoms, the Strike deals an additional 1d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage and an additional 1d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage to living creatures, or an additional 1d6 [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Toshigami Blossom

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 197

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +17; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) and one other common or regional language)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Sports Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17

**Int** +2, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +4

**Will** +17 

</column>

---        

_Wrestler's armbands_ are a set of _[armbands of athleticism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=409)_ that have gained their own boisterous sapience. Often, such items aren't created intentionally but rather are the result of a truly charismatic champion whose heart and body are unusually strong wearing the _armbands of athleticism_ on many adventures as their most important item. Eventually, the essence of their earnest and adventurous spirit has so thoroughly inundated the item it gains a matching spirit and will of its own. Such armbands view themselves as a tough, but fair, trainer for whomever inherits them next, inspiring you to new heights of athleticism. They also spoil for contests, telepathically calling out competitors on your behalf; typically the armbands won't challenge a foe you're unprepared to face, but particularly eager wrestler's armbands might have an unfortunate habit of overestimating your abilities and pushing you into a conflict that's beyond your proven capacity. These armbands have the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check;  **Effect** The armbands taunt a creature you're competing with, boasting of your prowess and attempting an Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) that creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wrestler's Armbands

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 204 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The _fan of the four winds_ once belonged to a sage of [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=8) and was passed down as a sacred relic for generations to those found worthy. Since that time, no one has unlocked the fan's full powers, though many devout priests have used it to aid them in their travels. A worthy member of this order might receive the fan as a reward, as could a suitable helper of the clergy. The fan might also have been lost, allowing for a new wielder to find it. The _fan of the four winds_ has the following activation. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You fan in a direction, and the fan casts [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143). If this casting is directed toward the sail of a vessel, it instead fills it with swirling air. The vessel gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. --- **Minor Gifts**: [feather steps](/Relics.aspx?ID=2), [wind barrier](/Relics.aspx?ID=3)
**Major Gifts**: [lightning storm](/Relics.aspx?ID=4), [propelling winds](/Relics.aspx?ID=5)
**Grand Gift**: [living storm](/Relics.aspx?ID=6)
name: Fan of the Four Winds

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 204 **Usage** worn --- Blessed by [Nivi Rhombodazzle](/Deities.aspx?ID=44), this striking silver necklace is adorned with sparkling sapphires that draw the eye of any who see it. Over the years, this necklace has appeared in many paintings and illustrations, usually around the neck of a carefree and daring adventurer or a stylish rake gambling vast sums of coin. Finding this relic is always seen as a sign of great fortune to come, but when that luck runs out, the necklace is lost just as quickly. Wearing the relic, you quickly realize how distracting it can be, granting you a +1 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks made to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) and on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks made to gamble or determine the outcome of a game of chance. --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [whammy](/Relics.aspx?ID=99)
**Major Gifts**: [clean luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=100), [perception filter](/Relics.aspx?ID=52)
**Grand Gift**: [against all odds](/Relics.aspx?ID=102)
name: Fortune's Favor

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Bulk** 3 --- Initially crafted by a gifted inventor who no longer had the use of their legs, the _inventor's chair_ is a [traveler's chair](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1356). It's a marvel of engineering, utilizing clockwork mechanisms to maneuver about. The chair comes equipped with [wheel blades](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1363), and portions of its frame contain [sterling artisan's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3). The _inventor's chair_ might be a family heirloom or a gift from someone who inherited it. It could instead sit unused in its inventor's abandoned estate, awaiting another user. --- **Minor Gifts**: [emotion surge](/Relics.aspx?ID=92), [muse](/Relics.aspx?ID=87) ([Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4))
**Major Gifts**: [emotion conduit](/Relics.aspx?ID=94), [enthralling](/Relics.aspx?ID=89)

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- Made of pure gold with platinum strings, the _phistophilus fiddle_ shouldn't be able to play a note—but play it does, so beautifully that it can move almost any audience. The fiddle was stolen from the [contract devil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=112) who created it, and the cunning fiend still wants to reclaim the instrument. Fiendish misfortunes often befall its owners, allowing it to switch hands. The fiddle is a handheld [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37). --- **Minor Gifts**: [little helper](/Relics.aspx?ID=33), [muse](/Relics.aspx?ID=87) ([Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12))
**Major Gifts**: [charming](/Relics.aspx?ID=88), [fiendish bargain](/Relics.aspx?ID=34)
**Grand Gift**: [artistic perfection](/Relics.aspx?ID=90) (Performance)

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A famed detective once owned the long, curved _sleuth's pipe_, taking it with them on investigations, keeping it in hand or mouth while cogitating. The pipe might have passed down through generations. Or, when an owner dies without heirs, it could go up for auction, be given to family friends, or remain at the scene of a crime to be found later. The _sleuth's pipe_ has sapience that eventually awakens, allowing the relic to act as a helpful assistant in investigations. Even while this sapience sleeps within the pipe, it grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85), and if you're an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=13), any checks you make to [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=544). --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [repository of knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51)
**Major Gifts**: [intelligent relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109), [steal luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)
**Grand Gift**: [mental bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54)
name: Sleuth's Pipe

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The origins of the _watch of lost ages_, a silver pocket watch, are enigmatic. Its construction matches no known culture, and its appearance shifts back and forth from battered and tarnished to shiny and new without cause or apparent reason. The watch exudes a sense of melancholy and loss, and anyone holding it is acutely aware of the impermanence of all things and the march of time, moving ever forward. This relic might show up in a mysterious ruin or spontaneously appear in a spellcaster's hand after they cast a time spell. The relic grants you a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about historical subjects. --- **Minor Gifts**: [fervor](/Relics.aspx?ID=93), [sands of the hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104)
**Major Gifts**: [fervent spell](/Relics.aspx?ID=95), [temporal pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106)
**Grand Gift**: [time reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108)
name: Watch of Lost Ages

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2411)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Astrolabe of Falling Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2412)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 79

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2411) is fashioned of magically attuned platinum plates and rings and is inset with carefully measured ruby gems. The astrolabe's back is engraved with helpful charts, a calendar, and a maker's mark. An _astrolabe of falling stars_ can be used as a mariner's astrolabe. In addition, it can produce the following magical effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a flick of your finger, you set the astrolabe's rule spinning, causing the inset rubies to become a blur. Choose a point within 100 feet. The _astrolabe of falling stars_ calls down a brief rain of meteorites in a 10-foot burst centered on that point, dealing 1d8 bludgeoning and 1d8 fire damage to creatures in the area (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Astrolabe of Falling Stars

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stargazer's Spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2413)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #188: They Watched the Stars](/Sources.aspx?ID=194) pg. 79

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate [spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=54) has a brass tube inscribed with constellations and green-tinted lens. It's often used by field astronomers to pick out greater details among stars and other celestial bodies. Like a typical spyglass, you can see eight times farther while looking through a _stargazer's spyglass_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are viewing the night sky with the spyglass; **Effect** You set your eye upon a star and think of someone to cast [_guiding star_](/Spells.aspx?ID=923).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stargazer's Spyglass

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Aether marbles create violent, lingering eddies of force that extend partially into unseen planes. When the bomb explodes, it deals the listed force damage and force [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. Many aether marbles grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

 Surfaces in the splash area are filled with twisting eddies of force, which are difficult terrain for [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures but not for corporeal ones. The force eddies in a square disperse after an incorporeal creature enters the square.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Telekinetic Converter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2417)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 75

**Price** 1,950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Copper cables run from these thick leather gloves to nodes that attach to the wearer's temples, allowing a spellcaster to convert the magic they normally cast to pure psychic intention. When you Cast a Spell using the _telekinetic converters_, you substitute any verbal spellcasting components for mental components of simple thought, granting the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait to the spell you're casting.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You cast [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) as an occult cantrip, heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You cast your choice of [_telekinetic haul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=332) or [_telekinetic maneuver_](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) as a 5th-level occult spell, consuming one of your unexpended spell slots of the same level as if you had used it to cast the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Telekinetic Converter

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs=""
>
[Psychic Colors Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2418)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 75

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This orange liquid lets you visibly detect the psychic energies that enable telekinesis and telepathy. For the next minute, you can sense if a creature you can see is using a telepathic or telekinetic ability, such as the [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) monster ability, spells like [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) or [_telepathic bond_](/Spells.aspx?ID=335), or similar abilities. You also can sense if an object or creature you can see is being manipulated or contacted by such an ability. Both the user and the target of the ability are outlined in faint shimmers of matching color.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Psychic Colors Elixir

However, the artificial brain is not robust, and the strain of the transfer quickly destroys it. The vat-grown brain has a counteract level of 5 and a +17 modifier on the roll.

>
[Ice Forge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2420)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 76

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L when not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While many of the magical wonders created by saumen kar have been destroyed, _ice forges_, while rare, still see use today among the few individuals who manage to discover and keep one of these portable treasures. When an _ice forge_ isn't activated, it appears to be a small anvil carved from a lump of ice no larger than an apple. While in this state, the _ice forge_ remains cold to the touch but doesn't melt in the presence of high temperatures. It can be used to chill a beverage in a small container, although those who venerate and respect these items' legacy would doubtless find this use to be insulting at best.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** You toss the _ice forge_ onto the ground, and it immediately expands in size, transforming into a fully functional forge that appears to be made of solid ice. Expanded, the _ice forge_ is 10 feet square and 10 feet high. While the forge itself is cold to the touch, it won't melt in higher temperatures, nor will it cause harm to someone who remains in contact with the chill surface for an extended period of time. The forge can be used to work metal as a normal forge (although fuel must be provided)—the heat created by its use does no harm to the _ice forge_, nor does it transmit to the surface it sits upon, making an _ice forge_ an ideal solution for those who seek to use forges in areas where open flames or smoke would be dangerous.

 You can return an _ice forge_ to its handheld anvil form by spending a single action to issue a verbal command, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait. Doing so immediately snuffs out any fire within the forge and leaves behind unused material or ashes. Once deactivated, the forge can't be activated again for 4 hours.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);    **Requirements** The _ice forge_ is in forge form; **Effect** The ice forge casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=54) (heightened to 5th level) to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ice Forge

Adlet spiritual leaders create this thick purple gel so that the dead can temporarily assume their appearance in life during ancestral worship ceremonies. Spreading it over the bones of an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature or a lifeless corpse causes the gel to congeal, forming a cosmetic layer that covers or restores any missing or compromised flesh until the body mimics its appearance in life. The dead creature's flesh looks healthy and whole. It gains a +2 circumstance bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to look like a living creature. The gel does not restore life or Hit Points and the flesh quickly rots away 8 hours after application.

Magical reagents slosh inside this rime-frosted pottery jar the size of a human head. Despite appearing to be rimed in hoarfrost, the jar is warm to the touch. Ilverani wizards create _rime jars_ to sustain themselves during the coldest winter nights.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** The jar draws off the cold and warms your body. If you have the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition caused by exposure to [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), it removes the condition. For 8 hours after applying the rime, you treat extreme cold as severe cold.

 When you activate this talisman, you gain a +2 status bonus to saves against enchantment magic with the mental trait for 1 minute. On the triggering save, if the outcome of the roll is a failure, you get a success instead, or if the outcome is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

<title

    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Mindsponge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2424)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #189: Dreamers of the Nameless Spires](/Sources.aspx?ID=195) pg. 77

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _mindsponge_ appears as a misshapen gray sponge that unsettlingly resembles a malformed human brain. Though pliable in all the same ways as a damp sponge, a _mindsponge_ has no ability to absorb water and, in fact, doesn't seem terribly porous at all. Instead, a _mindsponge_ is used to absorb thoughts and memories from living creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** The _mindsponge_ casts [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200), but only to erase a memory—a _mindsponge_ can't enhance memory clarity, alter memories, or add false memories.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);     **Effect** You use the _mindsponge_ to harvest the memories and mental energy of a specially prepared target as it dies. To prepare the target (who must be willing or helpless), you must perform a 10-minute surgery to expose a portion of the target's brain without killing them. This requires a successful DC 28 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) (master) check; if you fail this check, the target dies before you can use the _mindsponge_.

 Memories and psychic energy absorbed aren't taken from the target's soul but from energies temporarily infused within the body, so that a creature slain in this way who is later brought back to life retains their harvested memories and energy. The corpse of someone who has had their memories harvested is difficult to communicate with via [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=329)—treat the saving throw result against that spell as one degree of success lower than the actual result.

 A _mindsponge_ can store the memories and energy of up to four creatures at a time. Once full, it cannot be activated at all, other than to squeeze it as an Interact action with the envision trait, which causes all of the memories within to drip out as cold ectoplasm, which swiftly evaporates if not used or contained.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mindsponge

This ring contains a compartment beneath the bezel intended to hold a small amount of poison. You can determine the ring's true purpose with a successful DC 20 Perception check. Noticing the compartment while the ring is being worn is more difficult and typically requires a successful Perception check against the [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) DC of the person wearing the ring. You place [poison](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9) within the ring using the same method for applying poison to a weapon. You can release the ring's poison or consume it using an Interact action if you have a free hand. The ring's compartment is large enough to hold an effective amount of most poisons, but it's too small to hold a significant amount of other liquids, including potions and magical oils.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quick-Change Outfit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2449)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 82

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk**  varies
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A quick-change outfit is in fact two separate outfits sewn together. The specialized sewing technique allows you to switch quickly between the two outfits. You can use 3 consecutive Interact actions to slip out of the outfit, swap to the other side, and slip it back on. The two outfits can be of any kind (such as [ordinary clothing and fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=15)) and appear as one outfit on one side and the second outfit on the other. The outfit's appearance is purely superficial, so you don't receive any special benefits from an outfit (such as protection from [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) with winter clothing), though the GM might still apply specific benefits like the bonus to checks with high-fashion fine clothing. You can notice the odd features of a quick-change outfit (such as extra seams) with a successful DC 20 Perception check. The outfit's price is equal to double the price of the more expensive of the two outfits it mimics, and its Bulk is 1 higher than the highest Bulk values of the two outfits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Quick-Change Outfit

This satchel contains all the basic equipment found in a [climbing kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14) plus the specialized equipment—including a harness, belay device, and locking clips—needed for descents. When [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) down with a rappelling kit, you move twice as fast as usual based on your check result.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scroll Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2451)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 82

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These belts are specially designed to allow for writing along the inside surface. You can scribe a spell to a scroll belt, [Crafting](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) the [scroll](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=52) as normal. A scroll belt can only hold a single spell, and you must Cast the Spell before you can scribe another spell to the belt. You can't Cast a Spell from the belt while wearing it; you must remove the belt with an Interact action. A scroll belt acts as a mundane belt, but when a spell is scribed on it, any creature can immediately discern that an unattended belt holds magic. If you're wearing the belt, a creature can notice the belt holds a spell with a successful Perception check against your [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scroll Belt

>
[Eyecatcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2452)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 82

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a weapon

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An eyecatcher is a simple tassel, reflective basket hilt, or other accessory attached to a weapon to serve as a distraction. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) while using a weapon with an eyecatcher attached. An eyecatcher affects the balancing of a weapon, making it slightly more unwieldy. This increases the weapon's Bulk by 1 and grants a –1 penalty to damage rolls with the weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eyecatcher

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

Using fabrics and fashion accessories, you disguise your armor to look like everyday clothing. After applying this adjustment, you can attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) to hide the nature of your armor. You gain a +1 item bonus to this check. The added weight and care make it more difficult to move around in the armor, increasing the armor's check penalty by 1, its Strength entry value by 2, and its Bulk by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Subtle Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Anylength Rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2454)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 50-foot length of silk [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) is lightweight and appears to be inlaid with golden threads. The rope can hold up to 3,000 pounds and is particularly durable, with a Hardness of 5 and 24 Hit Points.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You command the rope to split, declaring a specific length or amount, such as “ten feet of rope,” “half of the rope,” and so on. The rope splits itself into the requested piece, leaving the remaining length intact. If the rope isn't long enough to provide the requested amount, the activation fails.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact
;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The rope is split into two or more pieces; **Effect** The rope attempts to reassemble itself. All pieces of the rope within 500 feet slither back toward you, moving 50 feet per round, over the span of 1 minute. Obstacles and other creatures can prevent a piece of rope from reaching you. Once all pieces reach you or the minute has passed, the pieces rejoin, becoming a single rope. The length of this reformed rope is equal to the total length of all the pieces together; if a piece is missing, the rope is now shorter than its original 50-foot length. Any pieces that didn't rejoin with the rest of the rope remain where they are after the minute passes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Anylength Rope (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Anylength Rope (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The rope is 75 feet long. You can activate the _moderate anylength rope_ once per hour instead of once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Anylength Rope (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The rope is 100 feet long. You can activate the _greater anylength rope_ without any frequency limit.
name: Anylength Rope

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blink Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2455)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ is etched with whirling portals, and a single blue sapphire adorns its hilt. It feels lightweight and is always slightly warm to the touch.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). The space you leave and the one you appear in are filled with puffs of smoke that make anyone within [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until they leave the smoke or until the end of your next turn, at which point the smoke dissipates. When used in strong winds, the smoke disperses immediately.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your melee attack roll with the _blink blade_ is a critical success;  **Effect** You and your target teleport to a space within 10 feet. The target arrives in a safe, open space of your choosing adjacent to your arrival space. This teleportation also creates puffs of smoke, as noted with the activation above.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blink Blade

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Boots of Free Running](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2456)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These comfortable and practical boots slip on easily and fill you with boundless energy. The treads of these boots provide exceptional traction, with improved grip on surfaces you would traditionally have difficulty traversing. While wearing the boots, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The traction of your boots improves, allowing you to run along vertical surfaces for 1 minute. When you Stride, you can run up solid vertical surfaces, like walls, at your full Speed. You must start your movement on a horizontal surface. If you end a Stride off the ground, you must Stride along the surface again until you reach a horizontal surface or you begin to fall (though you can [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95), if applicable). If you have means of walking on water, such as with [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) or similar abilities, you can also run along flimsy vertical surfaces, as well as vertical liquids such as a waterfall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Free Running (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 135 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Free Running (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2. You can activate the _moderate boots of free running_ once per hour instead of once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Boots of Free Running (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 84

**Price** 13,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3. You can activate the _greater boots of free running_ without a frequency limit.
name: Boots of Free Running

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cape of Grand Entrances](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2457)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This regal blue cape is crafted from a hefty yet elegant velvet embroidered with ornate silver stars and dragons. Falling in a cascade of fabric, the cape rustles and flows in all the right ways and hangs upon your shoulders in a way that flatters your form and figure. You draw the eyes of those around you and gain a +2 item bonus to your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a flourish of your cape, you make a grand entrance that draws the attention of those around you. You cast [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) with a DC of 25. The spell gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait and loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait. You Sustain the Spell by continuing to sweep the cape about.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cape of Grand Entrances

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L **Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item. --- This golden cape, embroidered with vibrant red sun rays, sparkles like sunlight reflecting off the ocean. **Activate** command, Interact; **Effect** The cape glows with the effects of 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171). You can Activate the cape again to Dismiss the effect. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You draw the cape and unleash a blinding flash of light. All enemies within 30 feet that can see you must attempt a DC 19 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute. The creature can spend an Interact action rubbing its eyes to end the blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 hour. Cape of Illumination (Lesser) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 135 gp **Bulk** L Cape of Illumination (Moderate) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 600 gp **Bulk** L --- The cape glows with the effects of 4th-level _light_. The save DC is 25. Cape of Illumination (Greater) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 5,750 gp **Bulk** L --- The cape glows with the effects of 7th-level _light_. The frequency is once per hour. The save DC is 34.
name: Cape of Illumination

markdown:
**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L **Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item. --- This golden cape, embroidered with vibrant red sun rays, sparkles like sunlight reflecting off the ocean. **Activate** command, Interact; **Effect** The cape glows with the effects of 1st-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171). You can Activate the cape again to Dismiss the effect. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You draw the cape and unleash a blinding flash of light. All enemies within 30 feet that can see you must attempt a DC 19 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute. The creature can spend an Interact action rubbing its eyes to end the blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 hour. Cape of Illumination (Lesser) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 135 gp **Bulk** L Cape of Illumination (Moderate) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 600 gp **Bulk** L --- The cape glows with the effects of 4th-level _light_. The save DC is 25. Cape of Illumination (Greater) **Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85 **Price** 5,750 gp **Bulk** L --- The cape glows with the effects of 7th-level _light_. The frequency is once per hour. The save DC is 34.
name: Cape of Illumination (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Charm of the Ordinary](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2459)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from a chunk of sturdy hardwood, this small charm is shaped like a broom, a wooden barrel, a lantern, or another ordinary item. When activated, the pendant becomes a full-size version of the item it represents, and you merge with the item with the effects of [_meld into stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), except the item doesn't need to be made of stone and doesn't require the volume to fit you and your worn or held possessions. While merged with the item, you can hear but can't see; unlike with _meld into stone_, you can't cast spells. The effect ends after ten minutes or when you Dismiss the effect, at which point you are forcibly expelled and the item you occupied becomes a mundane item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Charm of the Ordinary

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clinging Bubbles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2460)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small vial of viscous, clear, soapy solution comes with a wand, attached to the inside of the vial's cap, with a little loop at its end. A vial has enough solution to be used 10 times.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You dip the wand in the solution and blow on it. An improbably large cloud of bubbles emerges in a square adjacent to you. This cloud travels in a straight line, moving 10 feet at the end of your turns. The bubbles are thick and sticky, and any creature who enters the same space as the bubble cloud becomes shrouded in them, taking a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed. A creature covered in bubbles can use an Interact action to pop the bubbles and remove the penalty. The cloud can be redirected with strong winds. The cloud of bubbles has AC 5, 20 Hit Points, and weakness 5 to piercing and slashing. The bubbles pop naturally and harmlessly after 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clinging Bubbles (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clinging Bubbles (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The cloud of bubbles takes up a 5-foot burst. Creatures covered in bubbles take a –10-foot circumstance penalty. Each Interact action used to pop bubbles reduces the penalty by 5 feet. The cloud of bubbles has 30 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clinging Bubbles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The cloud of bubbles takes up a 10-foot burst. Creatures covered in bubbles take a –15-foot circumstance penalty. Each Interact action used to pop bubbles reduces the penalty by 5 feet. The cloud of bubbles has 40 Hit Points.
name: Clinging Bubbles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Diver's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2461)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather gloves fit snuggly, running up the length of your arm to your elbow. Each glove has an angular fin running along the outside edge of your arms, which forms a narrow triangle when your hands meet in a diving position. While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You would take [damage from falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) into water;  **Effect** The gloves create a hydrodynamic barrier around you, dispersing the damage that would be done to you during a high dive. You take damage as if the fall were half the distance, to a minimum fall of 5 feet. You then Swim with the effects of a critical success for the check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2. The gloves grant you a swim Speed equal to half of your land Speed.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Diver's Gloves (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 85

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3. The gloves grant you a swim Speed equal to your land Speed or 30 feet, whichever is higher.
name: Diver's Gloves

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You would take [damage from falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) into water;  **Effect** The gloves create a hydrodynamic barrier around you, dispersing the damage that would be done to you during a high dive. You take damage as if the fall were half the distance, to a minimum fall of 5 feet. You then Swim with the effects of a critical success for the check.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Horned Lion Amulet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2462)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 86

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You take fire damage.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small figurine of a horned lion sitting at attention is the size of a coin and carved from simple stone. The figurine can be fastened to a suit of armor as a charm or adornment. When you activate the amulet, the lion's eyes glow with flames, granting you fire resistance 10 against the triggering damage and a subsequent fire resistance 5 for 1 minute. If the triggering damage was due to [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), you immediately attempt a DC 10 flat check to recover from the persistent damage. The DC remains at 10 until the persistent fire damage ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Horned Lion Amulet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Parchment of Direct Message](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2463)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 86

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These two sheets resemble ordinary parchment, save for a simple seal burned into the corner. The two sheets are magically linked, though if either of the sheets is burnt, cut  in half or smaller, or otherwise destroyed, then the other sheet becomes an ordinary piece of parchment. When you write a message on one of the sheets, the text disappears over the span of 1 minute, leaving no ink, imprint, or any other trace of inscription behind. The message then appears on the linked piece of parchment. The magical link between the sheets only functions while both sheets are within 10 miles of each other. Outside of this range, any writing remains on the sheets as if they were mundane sheets of parchment, though this writing becomes erased if the parchment is activated.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You utter a specific magical phrase while holding one of the sheets of parchment. All writing disappears from the parchment.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Parchment of Direct Message

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pipe of Dancing Smoke](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2464)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate pipe is chiseled from a single bloodstone piece with wooden accents. When in use, the pipe gives off a vibrant red smoke that floats lazily in the air. These pipes are popular among bards and braggarts who like the ostentatious smoke it gives off—and who often use it to create raucous diversions or send indiscreet signals.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The smoke forms a bird that flies up to 120 feet in a direction of your choice. When it collides with a solid object or travels the full range, whichever is shorter, it explodes in a 20-foot burst of smoke with the effects of [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pipe of Dancing Smoke

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ring of Observation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2465)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple gold ring is engraved with eyes and is inlaid with a single amber-colored cat-eye gemstone. While the gemstone itself isn't magical, it gives off the eerie impression that it's watching you. A Firebrand makes use of this ring to determine if they've drawn the attention of locals and guards without the owner giving away the fact that they know others are watching. More powerful versions of the ring help Firebrands escape the sight of others when necessary.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The watchful eye of the cat-eye gemstone remains unmoving but feels as if it's surveying your surroundings. The eye continues this uncanny surveying for 1 minute. During this time, you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to focus on the ring. When you focus on the ring, it grows warm to the touch if you're being intentionally observed by creatures within 30 feet. The ring can only notice creatures that aren't [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from you and that are intentionally watching you. The ring ignores people who are part of a crowd but aren't actively paying attention to you, for example.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Observation (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Observation (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring surveys for 10 minutes and notices creatures out to 60 feet. The ring can cast [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) on you once per day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Observation (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

**Price** 1,160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring surveys for 1 hour and notices creatures out to 120 feet. The ring can cast either _invisibility_ or 4th-level _invisibility_ on you once per day.
name: Ring of Observation

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This simple gold ring is engraved with eyes and is inlaid with a single amber-colored cat-eye gemstone. While the gemstone itself isn't magical, it gives off the eerie impression that it's watching you. A Firebrand makes use of this ring to determine if they've drawn the attention of locals and guards without the owner giving away the fact that they know others are watching. More powerful versions of the ring help Firebrands escape the sight of others when necessary.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The watchful eye of the cat-eye gemstone remains unmoving but feels as if it's surveying your surroundings. The eye continues this uncanny surveying for 1 minute. During this time, you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to focus on the ring. When you focus on the ring, it grows warm to the touch if you're being intentionally observed by creatures within 30 feet. The ring can only notice creatures that aren't [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from you and that are intentionally watching you. The ring ignores people who are part of a crowd but aren't actively paying attention to you, for example.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Smuggler's Sack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2466)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This specialized version of a bag of holding appears to be a plain, leather bag that opens at the top, with a thin leather cord attached to cinch the sack closed. The sack's magic allows you to access either its mundane space or an extradimensional pocket. The mundane space inside the sack always holds 5 Bulk. The pocket's capacity varies with the type of sack. Anyone searching the bag can notice a thin, magical seam indicating the existence of the extradimensional space with a successful DC 18 Perception check.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You speak a secret command word. Depending on which word you use, the sack either opens up to the mundane sack or the extradimensional pocket. You can Interact to put items in or remove them as normal.
